docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-4.md:11116:9ccf94a fix(contracts): Phase 5 — ResourcesDeposited event order + tick + 4 MED (gemini-CA r1 on #193) (Closes #201) (#234)
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-4.md:11118:c7e4cbc feat(contracts): Phase 5.5 — deposit action (Closes #185) (#188)
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-4.md:11165:    BuildWall,
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-4.md:11166:    UpgradeBase,
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-4.md:11167:    UpgradeMonument,
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-4.md:11172:    UpgradeWall
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-4.md:11184:    event ResourcesDeposited(
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-4.md:11234:   412	A clansman must be at their own homebase and perform `deposit_resources` for carried resources to enter the clan vault.
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-4.md:11414:- Upgrade durations are wrong per the authoritative Phase 8 spec. The contract returns 2 ticks for `UpgradeWall`/`UpgradeBase` and 4 ticks for `UpgradeMonument` in [ClanWorld.sol:2130](/home/claude/code/clan-world/packages/contracts/src/ClanWorld.sol:2130) and [ClanWorld.sol:2134](/home/claude/code/clan-world/packages/contracts/src/ClanWorld.sol:2134), but §8 says all three build actions are “single-tick actions” and consume “one full action tick” at homebase in [clanworld_v4_spec.md:877](/home/claude/code/clan-world/docs/planning/V1/01%20Blockchain%20Game%20Spec/clanworld_v4_spec.md:877) and [clanworld_v4_spec.md:942](/home/claude/code/clan-world/docs/planning/V1/01%20Blockchain%20Game%20Spec/clanworld_v4_spec.md:942). The new tests lock in the mismatched timing instead of catching it in [MissionTiming.t.sol:130](/home/claude/code/clan-world/packages/contracts/test/MissionTiming.t.sol:130), [MissionTiming.t.sol:131](/home/claude/code/clan-world/packages/contracts/test/MissionTiming.t.sol:131), and [MissionTiming.t.sol:136](/home/claude/code/clan-world/packages/contracts/test/MissionTiming.t.sol:136).
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-4.md:11418:- `getClanScore()`/`getRankings()` are not actually “live” or derived-correct. `quoteLootValueSettled()` is still a raw passthrough in [ClanWorld.sol:2274](/home/claude/code/clan-world/packages/contracts/src/ClanWorld.sol:2274), and the score path reads committed storage directly in [ClanWorld.sol:2300](/home/claude/code/clan-world/packages/contracts/src/ClanWorld.sol:2300) through [ClanWorld.sol:2318](/home/claude/code/clan-world/packages/contracts/src/ClanWorld.sol:2318) and [ClanWorld.sol:2351](/home/claude/code/clan-world/packages/contracts/src/ClanWorld.sol:2351) through [ClanWorld.sol:2368](/home/claude/code/clan-world/packages/contracts/src/ClanWorld.sol:2368). That conflicts with the UI/indexer getter requirement that these reads simulate settlement via the derived path in [clanworld_v4_4_ui_indexer_getters.md:82](/home/claude/code/clan-world/docs/planning/V1/01%20Blockchain%20Game%20Spec/clanworld_v4_4_ui_indexer_getters.md:82) and [clanworld_v4_4_ui_indexer_getters.md:231](/home/claude/code/clan-world/docs/planning/V1/01%20Blockchain%20Game%20Spec/clanworld_v4_4_ui_indexer_getters.md:231). Pending deposits/upkeep/builds can therefore produce stale scores and stale ordering.
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-4.md:11426:- `getRankings()` silently truncates to the first 24 clan IDs in [ClanWorld.sol:2298](/home/claude/code/clan-world/packages/contracts/src/ClanWorld.sol:2298) through [ClanWorld.sol:2304](/home/claude/code/clan-world/packages/contracts/src/ClanWorld.sol:2304), but the interface still presents it as the ranking of “live clans” with no partial-results caveat in [IClanWorld.sol:773](/home/claude/code/clan-world/packages/contracts/src/IClanWorld.sol:773). It is masked by the current 12-clan mint cap in [ClanWorld.sol:1039](/home/claude/code/clan-world/packages/contracts/src/ClanWorld.sol:1039), but it is a future correctness cliff.
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-4.md:11428:- The contract now uses wall level as the fourth ranking key, matching [clanworld_v4_spec.md:956](/home/claude/code/clan-world/docs/planning/V1/01%20Blockchain%20Game%20Spec/clanworld_v4_spec.md:956) through [clanworld_v4_spec.md:960](/home/claude/code/clan-world/docs/planning/V1/01%20Blockchain%20Game%20Spec/clanworld_v4_spec.md:960), but the frontend spec still tells clients to sort only by monument level, earliest reach tick, then loot value in [clanworld_frontend_spec.md:169](/home/claude/code/clan-world/docs/planning/V1/02%20Frontend%20Spec/clanworld_frontend_spec.md:169) through [clanworld_frontend_spec.md:170](/home/claude/code/clan-world/docs/planning/V1/02%20Frontend%20Spec/clanworld_frontend_spec.md:170). Any client-side sort built from that doc will disagree with `getRankings()` on equal-loot ties.
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-4.md:11441:- `BuildWall` still bypasses the new queue-time reservation model, so wall progression can still happen through the old 1-tick settlement-time debit path. That reopens the vault race this PR is trying to close, and it can also strand reserved `UpgradeWall` funds if a legacy `BuildWall` reaches level 5 first. See [ClanWorld.sol:1670](/home/claude/code/clan-world/packages/contracts/src/ClanWorld.sol:1670), [ClanWorld.sol:765](/home/claude/code/clan-world/packages/contracts/src/ClanWorld.sol:765), [ClanWorld.sol:815](/home/claude/code/clan-world/packages/contracts/src/ClanWorld.sol:815), and [ClanWorld.sol:2138](/home/claude/code/clan-world/packages/contracts/src/ClanWorld.sol:2138).
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-4.md:11447:- `getClanScore()` and `getRankings()` are still using committed storage instead of the settled/derived path, so leaderboard results can be stale whenever a clan has pending lazy settlement. See [ClanWorld.sol:2274](/home/claude/code/clan-world/packages/contracts/src/ClanWorld.sol:2274), [ClanWorld.sol:2300](/home/claude/code/clan-world/packages/contracts/src/ClanWorld.sol:2300), and [ClanWorld.sol:2351](/home/claude/code/clan-world/packages/contracts/src/ClanWorld.sol:2351). That conflicts with the derived-getter requirements in [clanworld_v4_4_ui_indexer_getters.md:82](</home/claude/code/clan-world/docs/planning/V1/01 Blockchain Game Spec/clanworld_v4_4_ui_indexer_getters.md:82>) and [clanworld_v4_4_ui_indexer_getters.md:231](</home/claude/code/clan-world/docs/planning/V1/01 Blockchain Game Spec/clanworld_v4_4_ui_indexer_getters.md:231>).
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-4.md:11450:- Cross-type retasking can fail incorrectly with `ERR_MISSING_RESOURCES` because validation runs before the previous reservation is refunded, and each validator only credits back an existing reservation of the same type. Retasking `UpgradeWall` to `UpgradeBase`/`UpgradeMonument` or the reverse can therefore reject an order that should succeed after refund. See [ClanWorld.sol:1233](/home/claude/code/clan-world/packages/contracts/src/ClanWorld.sol:1233), [ClanWorld.sol:1252](/home/claude/code/clan-world/packages/contracts/src/ClanWorld.sol:1252), [ClanWorld.sol:1755](/home/claude/code/clan-world/packages/contracts/src/ClanWorld.sol:1755), [ClanWorld.sol:1812](/home/claude/code/clan-world/packages/contracts/src/ClanWorld.sol:1812), and [ClanWorld.sol:1880](/home/claude/code/clan-world/packages/contracts/src/ClanWorld.sol:1880).
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-4.md:11455:- Coverage is missing for the riskiest new paths: legacy `BuildWall`, travel-before-build, replacement refund, death refund, and cross-type replacement. The new suite only covers at-home happy-path queue/settle, insufficiency, max level, and clan isolation in [WallUpgrades.t.sol:109](/home/claude/code/clan-world/packages/contracts/test/WallUpgrades.t.sol:109), [BaseUpgrades.t.sol:112](/home/claude/code/clan-world/packages/contracts/test/BaseUpgrades.t.sol:112), and [MonumentUpgrades.t.sol:123](/home/claude/code/clan-world/packages/contracts/test/MonumentUpgrades.t.sol:123).
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-4.md:11460:- The wall system currently has two conflicting semantics in production: `BuildWall` is legacy immediate-settle, `UpgradeWall` is reserved queue-time spend. That split is the root of the nastiest wall bugs here.
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-4.md:11475:- `BuildWall` still bypasses the new queue-time reservation model, so wall progression can still happen through the old 1-tick settlement-time debit path. That reopens the vault race this PR is trying to close, and it can also strand reserved `UpgradeWall` funds if a legacy `BuildWall` reaches level 5 first. See [ClanWorld.sol:1670](/home/claude/code/clan-world/packages/contracts/src/ClanWorld.sol:1670), [ClanWorld.sol:765](/home/claude/code/clan-world/packages/contracts/src/ClanWorld.sol:765), [ClanWorld.sol:815](/home/claude/code/clan-world/packages/contracts/src/ClanWorld.sol:815), and [ClanWorld.sol:2138](/home/claude/code/clan-world/packages/contracts/src/ClanWorld.sol:2138).
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-4.md:11481:- `getClanScore()` and `getRankings()` are still using committed storage instead of the settled/derived path, so leaderboard results can be stale whenever a clan has pending lazy settlement. See [ClanWorld.sol:2274](/home/claude/code/clan-world/packages/contracts/src/ClanWorld.sol:2274), [ClanWorld.sol:2300](/home/claude/code/clan-world/packages/contracts/src/ClanWorld.sol:2300), and [ClanWorld.sol:2351](/home/claude/code/clan-world/packages/contracts/src/ClanWorld.sol:2351). That conflicts with the derived-getter requirements in [clanworld_v4_4_ui_indexer_getters.md:82](</home/claude/code/clan-world/docs/planning/V1/01 Blockchain Game Spec/clanworld_v4_4_ui_indexer_getters.md:82>) and [clanworld_v4_4_ui_indexer_getters.md:231](</home/claude/code/clan-world/docs/planning/V1/01 Blockchain Game Spec/clanworld_v4_4_ui_indexer_getters.md:231>).
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-4.md:11484:- Cross-type retasking can fail incorrectly with `ERR_MISSING_RESOURCES` because validation runs before the previous reservation is refunded, and each validator only credits back an existing reservation of the same type. Retasking `UpgradeWall` to `UpgradeBase`/`UpgradeMonument` or the reverse can therefore reject an order that should succeed after refund. See [ClanWorld.sol:1233](/home/claude/code/clan-world/packages/contracts/src/ClanWorld.sol:1233), [ClanWorld.sol:1252](/home/claude/code/clan-world/packages/contracts/src/ClanWorld.sol:1252), [ClanWorld.sol:1755](/home/claude/code/clan-world/packages/contracts/src/ClanWorld.sol:1755), [ClanWorld.sol:1812](/home/claude/code/clan-world/packages/contracts/src/ClanWorld.sol:1812), and [ClanWorld.sol:1880](/home/claude/code/clan-world/packages/contracts/src/ClanWorld.sol:1880).
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-4.md:11489:- Coverage is missing for the riskiest new paths: legacy `BuildWall`, travel-before-build, replacement refund, death refund, and cross-type replacement. The new suite only covers at-home happy-path queue/settle, insufficiency, max level, and clan isolation in [WallUpgrades.t.sol:109](/home/claude/code/clan-world/packages/contracts/test/WallUpgrades.t.sol:109), [BaseUpgrades.t.sol:112](/home/claude/code/clan-world/packages/contracts/test/BaseUpgrades.t.sol:112), and [MonumentUpgrades.t.sol:123](/home/claude/code/clan-world/packages/contracts/test/MonumentUpgrades.t.sol:123).

docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:3684:docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-opus-4-7.md:16:The new `_simulateSettleToTick` chain (`ClanWorld.sol:~1014-1505`) faithfully mirrors the storage-mutating settlement, but it **cannot emit events** (it's `view`). That means UIs/indexers that read `getDerivedClanState` or `getClanFullView` will see wall/base/monument upgrades, deposits, and resource yields **before** any matching `WallLevelChanged`, `BaseLevelChanged`, `MonumentLevelChanged`, `ResourcesGathered`, `ResourcesDeposited`, `ClanStarvationChanged`, or `MissionCompleted` event is logged. This was a pre-existing tradeoff for read-side simulation, but it introduces a new operational risk: any indexer (`apps/server/convex`) that reconciles state from events will silently disagree with the contract's view-function output until the next real `settleClan()` runs. **Recommendation:** add a comment block on the simulation cluster noting that derived state may "lead" the event log by up to ~200 ticks, and confirm the Convex indexer is event-driven (not view-poll-driven) to avoid double-counting.

docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:5261:docs/planning/clanworld_v4_spec.md:11:ClanWorld is an autonomous strategy game in which each player owns a homebase iNFT and an Elder agent that directs a small clan of workers across a shared world. Workers travel between regions, gather and deposit materials, defend bases, survive winter, trade assets, and compete to build the tallest monument.

docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:6081:docs/reviews/pr198-codereview-codex-5-4.md:10907:  Market I/O is using the wrong balance domain. Sells read/deduct `clan.vault*`, and buys credit `clan.vault*`, while submission still rejects buys based on worker carry capacity. Per the Phase 6 market spec, `market_sell` is from worker carry and `market_buy` returns to worker carry. As shipped, a clansman can liquidate home-vault inventory without hauling it to Unicorn Town, and bought goods become instantly eligible for upkeep/bandit theft without a deposit trip. The tests lock this in (`packages/contracts/test/ClanWorld.t.sol:775-888, 1013-1085, 1485-1490`).
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:6082:docs/reviews/pr198-codereview-codex-5-4.md:10939:  Market I/O is using the wrong balance domain. Sells read/deduct `clan.vault*`, and buys credit `clan.vault*`, while submission still rejects buys based on worker carry capacity. Per the Phase 6 market spec, `market_sell` is from worker carry and `market_buy` returns to worker carry. As shipped, a clansman can liquidate home-vault inventory without hauling it to Unicorn Town, and bought goods become instantly eligible for upkeep/bandit theft without a deposit trip. The tests lock this in (`packages/contracts/test/ClanWorld.t.sol:775-888, 1013-1085, 1485-1490`).

docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:6250:docs/reviews/pr198-codereview-codex-5-5.md:10325:- `packages/contracts/src/ClanWorld.sol:1656`, `1771`, `1824`, `1946` — Market trades bypass clansman carry and mutate clan vaults directly. Spec says `MarketSell` source is worker carry and `MarketBuy` destination is worker carry (`docs/planning/clanworld_v4_2_state_schema_interface_spec.md:565-585`). Current code lets a worker in Unicorn Town sell homebase vault resources without carrying them, and bought resources appear in the vault without a return/deposit trip. This also changes bandit loot/target timing because vault value changes immediately.
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:6251:docs/reviews/pr198-codereview-codex-5-5.md:10335:- `packages/contracts/test/HeartbeatOrdering.t.sol` encodes the vault-based market model: one clansman deposits into vault and another sells from vault in the same heartbeat. That protects heartbeat ordering, but it masks the carry-accounting regression above.
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:6252:docs/reviews/pr198-codereview-codex-5-5.md:10360:- `packages/contracts/src/ClanWorld.sol:1656`, `1771`, `1824`, `1946` — Market trades bypass clansman carry and mutate clan vaults directly. Spec says `MarketSell` source is worker carry and `MarketBuy` destination is worker carry (`docs/planning/clanworld_v4_2_state_schema_interface_spec.md:565-585`). Current code lets a worker in Unicorn Town sell homebase vault resources without carrying them, and bought resources appear in the vault without a return/deposit trip. This also changes bandit loot/target timing because vault value changes immediately.
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:6253:docs/reviews/pr198-codereview-codex-5-5.md:10370:- `packages/contracts/test/HeartbeatOrdering.t.sol` encodes the vault-based market model: one clansman deposits into vault and another sells from vault in the same heartbeat. That protects heartbeat ordering, but it masks the carry-accounting regression above.
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:6525:docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-opus-4-7.md:16:The new `_simulateSettleToTick` chain (`ClanWorld.sol:~1014-1505`) faithfully mirrors the storage-mutating settlement, but it **cannot emit events** (it's `view`). That means UIs/indexers that read `getDerivedClanState` or `getClanFullView` will see wall/base/monument upgrades, deposits, and resource yields **before** any matching `WallLevelChanged`, `BaseLevelChanged`, `MonumentLevelChanged`, `ResourcesGathered`, `ResourcesDeposited`, `ClanStarvationChanged`, or `MissionCompleted` event is logged. This was a pre-existing tradeoff for read-side simulation, but it introduces a new operational risk: any indexer (`apps/server/convex`) that reconciles state from events will silently disagree with the contract's view-function output until the next real `settleClan()` runs. **Recommendation:** add a comment block on the simulation cluster noting that derived state may "lead" the event log by up to ~200 ticks, and confirm the Convex indexer is event-driven (not view-poll-driven) to avoid double-counting.
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:6552:docs/planning/V1/02 Frontend Spec/clanworld_frontend_spec.md:195:| Resources become useful | World: wheelbarrow fill animation, deposit animation at base. Inspector: vault counter ticks up |

docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:8186:docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:3684:docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-opus-4-7.md:16:The new `_simulateSettleToTick` chain (`ClanWorld.sol:~1014-1505`) faithfully mirrors the storage-mutating settlement, but it **cannot emit events** (it's `view`). That means UIs/indexers that read `getDerivedClanState` or `getClanFullView` will see wall/base/monument upgrades, deposits, and resource yields **before** any matching `WallLevelChanged`, `BaseLevelChanged`, `MonumentLevelChanged`, `ResourcesGathered`, `ResourcesDeposited`, `ClanStarvationChanged`, or `MissionCompleted` event is logged. This was a pre-existing tradeoff for read-side simulation, but it introduces a new operational risk: any indexer (`apps/server/convex`) that reconciles state from events will silently disagree with the contract's view-function output until the next real `settleClan()` runs. **Recommendation:** add a comment block on the simulation cluster noting that derived state may "lead" the event log by up to ~200 ticks, and confirm the Convex indexer is event-driven (not view-poll-driven) to avoid double-counting.

docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:12520:docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:6250:docs/reviews/pr198-codereview-codex-5-5.md:10325:- `packages/contracts/src/ClanWorld.sol:1656`, `1771`, `1824`, `1946` — Market trades bypass clansman carry and mutate clan vaults directly. Spec says `MarketSell` source is worker carry and `MarketBuy` destination is worker carry (`docs/planning/clanworld_v4_2_state_schema_interface_spec.md:565-585`). Current code lets a worker in Unicorn Town sell homebase vault resources without carrying them, and bought resources appear in the vault without a return/deposit trip. This also changes bandit loot/target timing because vault value changes immediately.

Current code lets a worker in Unicorn Town sell homebase vault resources without carrying them, and bought resources appear in the vault without a return/deposit trip. This also changes bandit loot/target timing because vault value changes immediately.

docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:14016:Not merge-ready. The happy-path upgrade flow is mostly coherent, but the phase still has merge-blocking accounting and ranking issues: legacy `BuildWall` bypasses the new reservation model, queued reservations are not tied to the level transition they paid for, and ranking still scores raw committed storage instead of settled/derived state. I could not run Forge locally because `forge` is not installed, so this is static review plus parallel subagent review.
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:14020:1. `BuildWall` remains a second wall-upgrade path that bypasses Phase 8 accounting  
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:14021:Refs: `ClanWorld.sol` `_doBuilding`, `_tryBuildWall`, `_settleWallUpgrade`, `getActionDuration`.
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:14023:`UpgradeWall` uses queue-time reservation and duration `2`; legacy `BuildWall` still spends at settlement time, has duration `1`, emits only `WallLevelChanged`, and ignores pending wall reservations. At wall level 4, a clan can queue `UpgradeWall` for level 5, then also settle `BuildWall`; if `BuildWall` lands first, the reservation is later cleared and the upgrade fails at max level with no refund. Net effect: double payment for one final wall level.
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:14025:Fix by removing/rejecting `BuildWall`, or route it through the exact same reservation/settlement path as `UpgradeWall`.
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:14035:Refs: `quoteLootValueSettled`, `_getClanScore`, `getRankings`, `RankGetters.t.sol`.
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:14037:`quoteLootValueSettled()` still returns raw `_lootValueRaw(_clans[clanId])`, and `_getClanScore()` reads `_clans[clanId]` directly. That violates the Phase 8.4 / #261 expectation that leaderboard scoring uses settled/derived getters. A clan with unsettled upkeep, deposits, deaths, or vault changes can be mis-ranked until someone mutates settlement.
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:14044:Refs: `_processOrder`, `_validateUpgradeWallOrder`, `_validateUpgradeBaseOrder`, `_validateUpgradeMonumentOrder`.
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:14084:Not merge-ready. The happy-path upgrade flow is mostly coherent, but the phase still has merge-blocking accounting and ranking issues: legacy `BuildWall` bypasses the new reservation model, queued reservations are not tied to the level transition they paid for, and ranking still scores raw committed storage instead of settled/derived state. I could not run Forge locally because `forge` is not installed, so this is static review plus parallel subagent review.
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:14088:1. `BuildWall` remains a second wall-upgrade path that bypasses Phase 8 accounting  
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:14089:Refs: `ClanWorld.sol` `_doBuilding`, `_tryBuildWall`, `_settleWallUpgrade`, `getActionDuration`.
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:14091:`UpgradeWall` uses queue-time reservation and duration `2`; legacy `BuildWall` still spends at settlement time, has duration `1`, emits only `WallLevelChanged`, and ignores pending wall reservations. At wall level 4, a clan can queue `UpgradeWall` for level 5, then also settle `BuildWall`; if `BuildWall` lands first, the reservation is later cleared and the upgrade fails at max level with no refund. Net effect: double payment for one final wall level.
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:14093:Fix by removing/rejecting `BuildWall`, or route it through the exact same reservation/settlement path as `UpgradeWall`.
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:14103:Refs: `quoteLootValueSettled`, `_getClanScore`, `getRankings`, `RankGetters.t.sol`.
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:14105:`quoteLootValueSettled()` still returns raw `_lootValueRaw(_clans[clanId])`, and `_getClanScore()` reads `_clans[clanId]` directly. That violates the Phase 8.4 / #261 expectation that leaderboard scoring uses settled/derived getters. A clan with unsettled upkeep, deposits, deaths, or vault changes can be mis-ranked until someone mutates settlement.
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:14112:Refs: `_processOrder`, `_validateUpgradeWallOrder`, `_validateUpgradeBaseOrder`, `_validateUpgradeMonumentOrder`.

docs/planning/V1/02 Frontend Spec/clanworld_agent_runner_spec.md:326:                enum: ['ChopWood','MineIron','FishDocks','FishDeepSea','HarvestWheat','DepositResources','BuildWall','UpgradeBase','UpgradeMonument','DefendBase','MarketBuy','MarketSell','Wait']
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-opus-4-7.md:7:**Verdict: NEEDS FIXES — do not merge as-is.** Phase 8.1–8.4 ship the correct shape (queue-time vault deduction, mission-nonce-bound reservations, score/rank getters), but four HIGH-severity bugs land with this PR — three of which produce permanent, silent resource loss in well-reachable scenarios (cold death, starvation, BuildWall+UpgradeWall race, level-binding mismatch on cancellation). One MEDIUM-severity bug re-violates the #261 derived-getter contract that #230 was supposed to enforce. The score formula bit-packing and `getRankings` sort are correct and well-designed; the bugs are entirely in the reservation/refund lifecycle and in the choice to read raw committed storage from a documented "settled"-named getter. Cost tables match v4 spec §8 line-for-line. Durations diverge from spec ("single-tick") to 2/2/4 — likely intentional but unannounced.
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-opus-4-7.md:33:        m.active = false;   // <-- only DefendBase has cleanup; UpgradeWall/Base/Monument missed
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-opus-4-7.md:47:**Fix:** in `_markClansmanDead` (and the per-clansman loop in `_markClanDead`), check `m.action` against UpgradeWall/Base/Monument and call the refund helper before setting `m.active = false`. Or better: extract a `_invalidateActiveMission(cs, m)` helper and route every death/invalidation path through it so the refund-on-death contract has a single source of truth.
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-opus-4-7.md:49:### H2 — `BuildWall` and `UpgradeWall` are dual code paths for the same cost table; running both in flight burns resources
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-opus-4-7.md:51:`BuildWall` (existing, duration 1, no reservation, deducts at settle via `_tryBuildWall` at lines 901-933) and `UpgradeWall` (new, duration 2, reserves at queue) both increment `wallLevel` 0→5 against the **same cost table**. `_validateAction` lets BOTH through when at homebase, and `_validateUpgradeWallOrder` only consults `_pendingWallUpgradesByClan` — which `_tryBuildWall` does not increment.
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-opus-4-7.md:54:1. Clansman A queues `UpgradeWall`. Validator: planned=4, pending=0, OK. Reserve 50w+15i (the L4→5 cost). pending=1.
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-opus-4-7.md:55:2. Clansman B queues `BuildWall`. Validator: only homebase check — passes. **No reservation, no pending bump, no vault deduct.**
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-opus-4-7.md:56:3. B settles first (duration 1 vs UpgradeWall's 2). `_tryBuildWall`: `nextLevel = 4+1 = 5`, costs 50w+15i, vault has it (UpgradeWall's reservation already accounted), deduct 50w+15i, set wallLevel=5.
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-opus-4-7.md:61:**Fix:** either (a) remove `BuildWall` and `_tryBuildWall` entirely (recommended — UpgradeWall covers L0→L5), or (b) route `BuildWall` through the same reservation path so `_pendingWallUpgradesByClan` covers both.
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-opus-4-7.md:68:1. `monumentLevel = 5`, no pending. Clansman A queues UpgradeMonument. Reserve at planned-level 5: 150w+20i+80wh, **no blueprint**.
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-opus-4-7.md:69:2. Clansman B queues UpgradeMonument. Reserve at planned-level 6: 200w+25i+100wh+**1e18 blueprint** (`_monumentUpgradeCost(6)`).
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-opus-4-7.md:77:### H4 — `getClanScore`/`quoteLootValueSettled` read raw committed storage, regressing the #261/#230 derived-getter invariant [`ClanWorld.sol:2274-2277`, `:2346-2368`]
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-opus-4-7.md:83:function _getClanScore(uint32 clanId) internal view returns (...) {
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-opus-4-7.md:91:The PR's own NatSpec on `getClanScore` admits this: *"...does not simulate pending lazy settlement until derived settlement getters are fixed in #230."* But #230 was supposed to be the fix for #261, which #261 in turn closed by simulating settlement in derived getters. Phase 8.4 was explicitly tracked as "uses derived getters per #261 fix" in the review brief. This getter does the opposite.
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-opus-4-7.md:112:`_clearWallUpgradeReservation` only deletes the reservation struct and decrements the pending counter — it does NOT credit vault. Currently this resource-burn path is unreachable in normal play (validator's pending counter prevents reserving past max with ≤ 4 clansmen per clan), but **Phase 9 attack-resolution will introduce wall-destruction writes**, creating a real race: queue UpgradeWall at L4 → bandit destroys wall to L3 between queue and settle → wall is back at L4 (then settle overwrites with `old+1=4` which is fine actually).
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-opus-4-7.md:114:The actual reachable bad case is via H1 (BuildWall steals the slot) and via H2 (early reservation cancellation desyncs pending count vs actual level). **Even setting H1/H2 aside, the defensive coding fix is trivial:** swap order so the cap check runs first, or change `_clearReservation` semantics so the failure path refunds instead of burning. I'd recommend ordering: nonce-check → cap-check → clear-reservation → emit (so any bail returns resources via `_refund*Reservation`).
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-opus-4-7.md:129:### M3 — Cross-type reassignment (e.g. UpgradeWall → UpgradeBase) validates against pre-refund vault, can spuriously reject [`ClanWorld.sol:1233`, `:1251-1268`, `:1806-1828`]
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-opus-4-7.md:131:`_validateAction` runs at line 1233, BEFORE `_refundWallUpgradeReservation` at line 1252. So switching a clansman from a queued UpgradeWall to a fresh UpgradeBase can return `ERR_MISSING_RESOURCES` even when, after refund, the vault would be sufficient. Workaround is "submit Wait first, then base" — but it's a bad UX seam for Elder agents.
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-opus-4-7.md:133:**Fix:** in `_validateUpgradeBaseOrder`, also synthesize the vault refund of any active wall/monument reservation for the same clansman. Or refactor: refund FIRST in `_processOrder` at the point of "we're definitely re-tasking", then validate the new action.
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-opus-4-7.md:139:- UpgradeBase reuses `UPGRADE_WALL_DURATION_TICKS = 2`
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-opus-4-7.md:141:- BuildWall = 1 (matches spec)
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-opus-4-7.md:171:### M7 — `ResourcesDeposited` event ABI break (uint64 atTick → uint32 tick, field renames) [`IClanWorld.sol:536-544`]
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-opus-4-7.md:175:event ResourcesDeposited(uint32 indexed clanId, uint32 indexed clansmanId,
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-opus-4-7.md:178:event ResourcesDeposited(uint32 indexed clanId, uint32 indexed clansmanId,
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-opus-4-7.md:196:The diff changes only `DepositResources` to return `ERR_INVALID_REGION` while `BuildWall`/`UpgradeWall`/`UpgradeBase`/`UpgradeMonument` (which have the same homebase requirement) continue to return `ERR_NOT_AT_HOMEBASE`. Either both should use ERR_NOT_AT_HOMEBASE (the more specific code) or both ERR_INVALID_REGION. The split is a code smell. Test was updated to match — but the inconsistency remains.
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-opus-4-7.md:207:### L4 — `_allClanIds` is append-only; dead clans persist in scan, wasting gas on `getRankings` [`ClanWorld.sol:69`, `:2310-2312`]
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-opus-4-7.md:219:### L7 — No test covers reservation refund on death, on cross-type retask, or on the BuildWall/UpgradeWall race
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-opus-4-7.md:221:Phase 8 tests verify happy-path queue, max-level rejection, isolation across clans, and cost-table sampling. They do NOT cover: (a) cold-damage death with active upgrade (would catch H1), (b) UpgradeWall→UpgradeBase swap (would surface M3), (c) BuildWall + UpgradeWall same-clan racing (would catch H2). Two-clansman parallel upgrade interaction is also untested. Hackathon discipline tolerates minimal tests, but H1/H2 are real bugs that a 30-line happy-path test would catch.
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-opus-4-7.md:231:- **Spec compliance on §8 cost tables is line-perfect** — wall L1=20w, L2=35w, L3=30w+5i, L4=40w+10i, L5=50w+15i; base L2=40w+20wh through L5=100w+15i+50wh; monument L1=30w+20wh through L6=150w+20i+80wh, L7-10=200w+25i+100wh+1e18 blueprint. No drift here. Drift is on durations only (M4) and on ResourcesDeposited event shape (M7).

docs/planning/V1/01 Blockchain Game Spec/clanworld_v4_spec.md:11:ClanWorld is an autonomous strategy game in which each player owns a homebase iNFT and an Elder agent that directs a small clan of workers across a shared world. Workers travel between regions, gather and deposit materials, defend bases, survive winter, trade assets, and compete to build the tallest monument.

docs/planning/V1/01 Blockchain Game Spec/clanworld_v4_spec.md:284:- deposit resources, then wait at Homebase
docs/planning/V1/01 Blockchain Game Spec/clanworld_v4_spec.md:313:- deposit resources
docs/planning/V1/01 Blockchain Game Spec/clanworld_v4_spec.md:409:Carried resources do **not** count until deposited.
docs/planning/V1/01 Blockchain Game Spec/clanworld_v4_spec.md:412:A clansman must be at their own homebase and perform `deposit_resources` for carried resources to enter the clan vault.
docs/planning/V1/01 Blockchain Game Spec/clanworld_v4_2_state_schema_interface_spec.md:817:### 12.5 Just-in-time deposit rule
docs/planning/V1/01 Blockchain Game Spec/clanworld_v4_2_state_schema_interface_spec.md:818:Because upkeep is applied before deposit effects, a worker arriving home with wood/food in carry does **not** save the base from that same tick’s winter burn or upkeep shortage.

docs/reviews/r2-phase10/pr250-codereview-opus-4-7.md:126:`ClanWorld.sol:565-591`. Vaults are wiped (`vaultWood/Wheat/Fish/Iron = 0`), but per-clansman `carryWood/Wheat/Fish/Iron` on the now-dead clansmen is preserved. Spec is silent on this. Inconsistent with vault wipe; harmless because dead clansmen can't deposit, but a future `getClanFullView` consumer might surface phantom carry. Either wipe or document the invariant.

docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-opus-4-7.md:16:The new `_simulateSettleToTick` chain (`ClanWorld.sol:~1014-1505`) faithfully mirrors the storage-mutating settlement, but it **cannot emit events** (it's `view`). That means UIs/indexers that read `getDerivedClanState` or `getClanFullView` will see wall/base/monument upgrades, deposits, and resource yields **before** any matching `WallLevelChanged`, `BaseLevelChanged`, `MonumentLevelChanged`, `ResourcesGathered`, `ResourcesDeposited`, `ClanStarvationChanged`, or `MissionCompleted` event is logged. This was a pre-existing tradeoff for read-side simulation, but it introduces a new operational risk: any indexer (`apps/server/convex`) that reconciles state from events will silently disagree with the contract's view-function output until the next real `settleClan()` runs. **Recommendation:** add a comment block on the simulation cluster noting that derived state may "lead" the event log by up to ~200 ticks, and confirm the Convex indexer is event-driven (not view-poll-driven) to avoid double-counting.
docs/planning/clanworld_v4_2_state_schema_interface_spec.md:817:### 12.5 Just-in-time deposit rule
docs/planning/clanworld_v4_2_state_schema_interface_spec.md:818:Because upkeep is applied before deposit effects, a worker arriving home with wood/food in carry does **not** save the base from that same tick’s winter burn or upkeep shortage.
docs/planning/clanworld_v4_2_state_schema_interface_spec.md:859:event ResourcesDeposited(uint32 indexed clanId, uint32 indexed clansmanId, uint256 wood, uint256 iron, uint256 wheat, uint256 fish);

docs/reviews/pr198-r2-codereview-codex-5-4.md:2319:+        emit IClanWorldEvents.ResourcesDeposited(clanId, csId, 5e18, 2e18, 1e18, 3e18, 1);
docs/reviews/pr198-r2-codereview-codex-5-4.md:3297:-        // Advance until the one-tick deposit duration has closed.
docs/reviews/pr198-r2-codereview-codex-5-4.md:3637:     //   - Heartbeat at T0+2: Step 1 deposits cs0 carry wood to vault, Step 2 sells
docs/reviews/pr198-r2-codereview-codex-5-4.md:3655:         assertEq(uint8(r0[0].status), uint8(StatusCode.OK), "deposit order must succeed");
docs/reviews/pr198-r2-codereview-codex-5-4.md:3656:         Mission memory depositMission = world.getActiveMission(csId0);
docs/reviews/pr198-r2-codereview-codex-5-4.md:3657:-        assertEq(depositMission.settlesAtTick, t0 + 2, "deposit settlesAtTick must be t0+2");
docs/reviews/pr198-r2-codereview-codex-5-4.md:3658:+        assertEq(depositMission.settlesAtTick, t0 + 3, "deposit settlesAtTick must be t0+3");
docs/reviews/pr198-r2-codereview-codex-5-4.md:3661:-        // executeAtTick = t0+2. Same tick as deposit settles.
docs/reviews/pr198-r2-codereview-codex-5-4.md:3662:+        // settlesAtTick = t0+3. Same tick as deposit settles.
docs/reviews/pr198-r2-codereview-codex-5-4.md:3670:+        // Give cs0 carry wood (for deposit) and cs1 carry wood (for sell).
docs/reviews/pr198-r2-codereview-codex-5-4.md:3680:-        //   Step 1: _settleCompletingMissions(t0+2) → deposit fires, cs0 carry 10e18 → vault
docs/reviews/pr198-r2-codereview-codex-5-4.md:3685:+        //   Step 1: _settleCompletingMissions(t0+3) → deposit fires, cs0 carry 10e18 → vault
docs/reviews/pr198-r2-codereview-codex-5-4.md:3695:-    //   - cs0 placed at Mountains, deposits to Forest homebase: settlesAtTick = T0+2
docs/reviews/pr198-r2-codereview-codex-5-4.md:3698:+    //   - cs0 placed at Unicorn Town, deposits to Forest homebase: settlesAtTick = T0+3
docs/reviews/pr198-r2-codereview-codex-5-4.md:3718:         assertEq(uint8(r0[0].status), uint8(StatusCode.OK), "deposit must succeed");
docs/reviews/pr198-r2-codereview-codex-5-4.md:3719:-        assertEq(world.getActiveMission(csId0).settlesAtTick, t0 + 2, "deposit settlesAtTick must be t0+2");
docs/reviews/pr198-r2-codereview-codex-5-4.md:3720:+        assertEq(world.getActiveMission(csId0).settlesAtTick, t0 + 3, "deposit settlesAtTick must be t0+3");
docs/reviews/pr198-r2-codereview-codex-5-4.md:3740:-        // Both cs0 deposit (settled at T0+2) and cs1 sell (at T0+2) must have fired.
docs/reviews/pr198-r2-codereview-codex-5-4.md:3741:+        // Both cs0 deposit (settled at T0+3) and cs1 sell (at T0+3) must have fired.
docs/reviews/pr198-r2-codereview-codex-5-4.md:3742:         assertEq(world.getClansman(csId0).carryWood, 0, "deposit settled: carry cleared");
docs/reviews/pr198-r2-codereview-codex-5-4.md:3744:         assertFalse(world.getActiveMission(csId0).active, "deposit mission must be complete");

docs/reviews/pr198-r2-codereview-codex-5-4.md:5440:   731	    function _tryBuildWall(Clan storage clan, uint32 clanId, uint64 tick) internal returns (bool) {
docs/reviews/pr198-r2-codereview-codex-5-4.md:6448:  2038	        // BuildWall / UpgradeBase / UpgradeMonument: must go to homebase
docs/reviews/pr198-r2-codereview-codex-5-4.md:6449:  2039	        if (action == ActionType.BuildWall || action == ActionType.UpgradeBase || action == ActionType.UpgradeMonument)
docs/reviews/pr198-r2-codereview-codex-5-4.md:6684:  2274	            action == ActionType.BuildWall || action == ActionType.UpgradeBase || action == ActionType.UpgradeMonument
docs/reviews/pr198-r2-codereview-codex-5-4.md:6992:The next pass is on heartbeat ordering and mission settlement. I’m checking whether enqueuing at resolve-time changes any same-tick behavior around deposits, bandits, or interrupted missions, and whether the new queue model left behind any liveness or duplication edges.
docs/reviews/pr198-r2-codereview-codex-5-4.md:7734:   191	    //   - Heartbeat at T0+2: Step 1 deposits cs0 carry wood to vault, Step 2 sells
docs/reviews/pr198-r2-codereview-codex-5-4.md:7753:   210	        assertEq(uint8(r0[0].status), uint8(StatusCode.OK), "deposit order must succeed");
docs/reviews/pr198-r2-codereview-codex-5-4.md:7754:   211	        Mission memory depositMission = world.getActiveMission(csId0);
docs/reviews/pr198-r2-codereview-codex-5-4.md:7755:   212	        assertEq(depositMission.settlesAtTick, t0 + 3, "deposit settlesAtTick must be t0+3");
docs/reviews/pr198-r2-codereview-codex-5-4.md:7758:   215	        // settlesAtTick = t0+3. Same tick as deposit settles.
docs/reviews/pr198-r2-codereview-codex-5-4.md:7765:   222	        // Give cs0 carry wood (for deposit) and cs1 carry wood (for sell).
docs/reviews/pr198-r2-codereview-codex-5-4.md:7775:   232	        //   Step 1: _settleCompletingMissions(t0+3) → deposit fires, cs0 carry 10e18 → vault
docs/reviews/pr198-r2-codereview-codex-5-4.md:7781:   238	        assertEq(world.getClansman(csId0).carryWood, 0, "cs0 carry wood cleared by deposit");
docs/reviews/pr198-r2-codereview-codex-5-4.md:7782:   239	        assertFalse(world.getActiveMission(csId0).active, "deposit mission must be complete");
docs/reviews/pr198-r2-codereview-codex-5-4.md:7865:   322	    //   - cs0 placed at Unicorn Town, deposits to Forest homebase: settlesAtTick = T0+3
docs/reviews/pr198-r2-codereview-codex-5-4.md:7883:   340	        assertEq(uint8(r0[0].status), uint8(StatusCode.OK), "deposit must succeed");
docs/reviews/pr198-r2-codereview-codex-5-4.md:7884:   341	        assertEq(world.getActiveMission(csId0).settlesAtTick, t0 + 3, "deposit settlesAtTick must be t0+3");
docs/reviews/pr198-r2-codereview-codex-5-4.md:7899:   356	        // Both cs0 deposit (settled at T0+3) and cs1 sell (at T0+3) must have fired.
docs/reviews/pr198-r2-codereview-codex-5-4.md:7900:   357	        assertEq(world.getClansman(csId0).carryWood, 0, "deposit settled: carry cleared");
docs/reviews/pr198-r2-codereview-codex-5-4.md:7902:   359	        assertFalse(world.getActiveMission(csId0).active, "deposit mission must be complete");
docs/reviews/pr198-r2-codereview-codex-5-4.md:7984:docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-4.md:6274:docs/reviews/pr198-codereview-codex-5-4.md:10907:  Market I/O is using the wrong balance domain. Sells read/deduct `clan.vault*`, and buys credit `clan.vault*`, while submission still rejects buys based on worker carry capacity. Per the Phase 6 market spec, `market_sell` is from worker carry and `market_buy` returns to worker carry. As shipped, a clansman can liquidate home-vault inventory without hauling it to Unicorn Town, and bought goods become instantly eligible for upkeep/bandit theft without a deposit trip. The tests lock this in (`packages/contracts/test/ClanWorld.t.sol:775-888, 1013-1085, 1485-1490`).

Sells read/deduct `clan.vault*`, and buys credit `clan.vault*`, while submission still rejects buys based on worker carry capacity. As shipped, a clansman can liquidate home-vault inventory without hauling it to Unicorn Town, and bought goods become instantly eligible for upkeep/bandit theft without a deposit trip. The tests lock this in (`packages/contracts/test/ClanWorld.t.sol:775-888, 1013-1085, 1485-1490`).

docs/reviews/pr198-r2-codereview-codex-5-4.md:8860:docs/reviews/r2-phase6/pr198-codereview-codex-5-5.md:10325:- `packages/contracts/src/ClanWorld.sol:1656`, `1771`, `1824`, `1946` — Market trades bypass clansman carry and mutate clan vaults directly. Spec says `MarketSell` source is worker carry and `MarketBuy` destination is worker carry (`docs/planning/clanworld_v4_2_state_schema_interface_spec.md:565-585`). Current code lets a worker in Unicorn Town sell homebase vault resources without carrying them, and bought resources appear in the vault without a return/deposit trip. This also changes bandit loot/target timing because vault value changes immediately.
docs/reviews/pr198-r2-codereview-codex-5-4.md:8862:docs/reviews/r2-phase6/pr198-codereview-codex-5-5.md:10360:- `packages/contracts/src/ClanWorld.sol:1656`, `1771`, `1824`, `1946` — Market trades bypass clansman carry and mutate clan vaults directly. Spec says `MarketSell` source is worker carry and `MarketBuy` destination is worker carry (`docs/planning/clanworld_v4_2_state_schema_interface_spec.md:565-585`). Current code lets a worker in Unicorn Town sell homebase vault resources without carrying them, and bought resources appear in the vault without a return/deposit trip. This also changes bandit loot/target timing because vault value changes immediately.
docs/reviews/pr198-r2-codereview-codex-5-4.md:8972:docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-opus-4-7.md:112:`_clearWallUpgradeReservation` only deletes the reservation struct and decrements the pending counter — it does NOT credit vault. Currently this resource-burn path is unreachable in normal play (validator's pending counter prevents reserving past max with ≤ 4 clansmen per clan), but **Phase 9 attack-resolution will introduce wall-destruction writes**, creating a real race: queue UpgradeWall at L4 → bandit destroys wall to L3 between queue and settle → wall is back at L4 (then settle overwrites with `old+1=4` which is fine actually).

docs/reviews/pr198-r2-codereview-codex-5-4.md:14233:- [ClanWorld.sol](/home/claude/code/clan-world/packages/contracts/src/ClanWorld.sol:668), [ClanWorld.sol](/home/claude/code/clan-world/packages/contracts/src/ClanWorld.sol:1858), [ClanWorld.sol](/home/claude/code/clan-world/packages/contracts/src/ClanWorld.sol:2184) — The carry-based rewrite removed vault-backed selling without adding any production `vault -> carry` path. `DepositResources` is only `carry -> vault`, `MarketSell` now always debits `carry`, and `transferVaultResource()` still reverts. In practice that means starter/deposited resources are trapped in the vault and cannot be sold, and the intended wheelbarrow anti-bandit mechanic is not actually implementable.
docs/reviews/pr198-r2-codereview-codex-5-4.md:14259:- [ClanWorld.sol](/home/claude/code/clan-world/packages/contracts/src/ClanWorld.sol:668), [ClanWorld.sol](/home/claude/code/clan-world/packages/contracts/src/ClanWorld.sol:1858), [ClanWorld.sol](/home/claude/code/clan-world/packages/contracts/src/ClanWorld.sol:2184) — The carry-based rewrite removed vault-backed selling without adding any production `vault -> carry` path. `DepositResources` is only `carry -> vault`, `MarketSell` now always debits `carry`, and `transferVaultResource()` still reverts. In practice that means starter/deposited resources are trapped in the vault and cannot be sold, and the intended wheelbarrow anti-bandit mechanic is not actually implementable.

docs/reviews/r2-phase6/pr198-codereview-opus-4-7.md:65:### L1 — `ResourcesDeposited.tick` is `uint32` (event ABI breaking change with cast)
docs/reviews/r2-phase6/pr198-codereview-opus-4-7.md:85:### L6 — Empty deposit silent no-op (no zero-delta event)
docs/reviews/r2-phase6/pr198-codereview-opus-4-7.md:87:`_doDeposit` (line 678) returns silently without emitting. Documented in code, but Convex `getMissionTiming`/event consumers that rely on always-on deposit events for "mission complete" need a different signal (`MissionCompleted` does fire from `_completeMission`, so OK in practice).
docs/reviews/r2-phase6/pr198-codereview-opus-4-7.md:91:- **Heartbeat ordering is correct.** Settle (step 1) → market exec (step 2) → world events (step 4) → tick increment (step 5) is verified by `HeartbeatOrdering.t.sol::test_heartbeat_settlementBeforeMarket` and `test_heartbeat_multipleStepsInOneTick`. Same-tick deposit-then-sell works because deposit's `_addToVault` happens before the queued sell drains the vault.
docs/reviews/r2-phase6/pr198-codereview-opus-4-7.md:96:- **Treasury seeding integrity:** the `_transferSeed → MinimalERC20.transferFrom` chain plus pool's `balanceOf` check at `seed()` is correct — pool reserves cannot exceed actually-deposited balances.
docs/reviews/r2-phase6/pr198-codereview-codex-5-5.md:518:       "name": "ResourcesDeposited",
docs/reviews/r2-phase6/pr198-codereview-codex-5-5.md:837:+            // Empty deposits are silent no-ops; no zero-delta event for indexers to process.
docs/reviews/r2-phase6/pr198-codereview-codex-5-5.md:865:-        emit ResourcesDeposited(clanId, cs.clansmanId, w, ir, wh, fi, tick);
docs/reviews/r2-phase6/pr198-codereview-codex-5-5.md:866:+        emit ResourcesDeposited(clanId, cs.clansmanId, woodDelta, ironDelta, wheatDelta, fishDelta, tick);
docs/reviews/r2-phase6/pr198-codereview-codex-5-5.md:1602:         // BuildWall / UpgradeBase / UpgradeMonument: must go to homebase

docs/reviews/r2-phase6/pr198-codereview-codex-5-5.md:1691:-                || action == ActionType.UpgradeMonument || action == ActionType.MarketBuy
docs/reviews/r2-phase6/pr198-codereview-codex-5-5.md:1693:+            action == ActionType.BuildWall || action == ActionType.UpgradeBase || action == ActionType.UpgradeMonument
docs/reviews/r2-phase6/pr198-codereview-codex-5-5.md:1802:     event ResourcesDeposited(
docs/reviews/r2-phase6/pr198-codereview-codex-5-5.md:2195:-        // Advance travel back to homebase + deposit duration.
docs/reviews/r2-phase6/pr198-codereview-codex-5-5.md:2196:+        // Advance through travel back to homebase and the deposit's 1-tick transfer.

docs/reviews/r2-phase6/pr198-codereview-codex-5-5.md:10325:- `packages/contracts/src/ClanWorld.sol:1656`, `1771`, `1824`, `1946` — Market trades bypass clansman carry and mutate clan vaults directly. Spec says `MarketSell` source is worker carry and `MarketBuy` destination is worker carry (`docs/planning/clanworld_v4_2_state_schema_interface_spec.md:565-585`). Current code lets a worker in Unicorn Town sell homebase vault resources without carrying them, and bought resources appear in the vault without a return/deposit trip. This also changes bandit loot/target timing because vault value changes immediately.
docs/reviews/r2-phase6/pr198-codereview-codex-5-5.md:10335:- `packages/contracts/test/HeartbeatOrdering.t.sol` encodes the vault-based market model: one clansman deposits into vault and another sells from vault in the same heartbeat. That protects heartbeat ordering, but it masks the carry-accounting regression above.
docs/reviews/r2-phase6/pr198-codereview-codex-5-5.md:10360:- `packages/contracts/src/ClanWorld.sol:1656`, `1771`, `1824`, `1946` — Market trades bypass clansman carry and mutate clan vaults directly. Spec says `MarketSell` source is worker carry and `MarketBuy` destination is worker carry (`docs/planning/clanworld_v4_2_state_schema_interface_spec.md:565-585`). Current code lets a worker in Unicorn Town sell homebase vault resources without carrying them, and bought resources appear in the vault without a return/deposit trip. This also changes bandit loot/target timing because vault value changes immediately.
docs/reviews/r2-phase6/pr198-codereview-codex-5-5.md:10370:- `packages/contracts/test/HeartbeatOrdering.t.sol` encodes the vault-based market model: one clansman deposits into vault and another sells from vault in the same heartbeat. That protects heartbeat ordering, but it masks the carry-accounting regression above.

docs/reviews/r2-phase6/pr198-codereview-codex-5-4.md:9421:docs/planning/V1/01 Blockchain Game Spec/clanworld_v4_2_state_schema_interface_spec.md:818:Because upkeep is applied before deposit effects, a worker arriving home with wood/food in carry does **not** save the base from that same tick’s winter burn or upkeep shortage.

docs/reviews/r2-phase6/pr198-codereview-codex-5-4.md:10907:  Market I/O is using the wrong balance domain. Sells read/deduct `clan.vault*`, and buys credit `clan.vault*`, while submission still rejects buys based on worker carry capacity. Per the Phase 6 market spec, `market_sell` is from worker carry and `market_buy` returns to worker carry. As shipped, a clansman can liquidate home-vault inventory without hauling it to Unicorn Town, and bought goods become instantly eligible for upkeep/bandit theft without a deposit trip. The tests lock this in (`packages/contracts/test/ClanWorld.t.sol:775-888, 1013-1085, 1485-1490`).
docs/reviews/r2-phase6/pr198-codereview-codex-5-4.md:10925:- The biggest integrated consequence is cross-phase: routing market buys/sells through vault balances instead of worker carry materially changes Phase 5 deposit logistics and Phase 9 bandit timing.
docs/reviews/r2-phase6/pr198-codereview-codex-5-4.md:10939:  Market I/O is using the wrong balance domain. Sells read/deduct `clan.vault*`, and buys credit `clan.vault*`, while submission still rejects buys based on worker carry capacity. Per the Phase 6 market spec, `market_sell` is from worker carry and `market_buy` returns to worker carry. As shipped, a clansman can liquidate home-vault inventory without hauling it to Unicorn Town, and bought goods become instantly eligible for upkeep/bandit theft without a deposit trip. The tests lock this in (`packages/contracts/test/ClanWorld.t.sol:775-888, 1013-1085, 1485-1490`).
docs/reviews/r2-phase6/pr198-codereview-codex-5-4.md:10957:- The biggest integrated consequence is cross-phase: routing market buys/sells through vault balances instead of worker carry materially changes Phase 5 deposit logistics and Phase 9 bandit timing.

Per the Phase 6 market spec, `market_sell` is from worker carry and `market_buy` returns to worker carry. As shipped, a clansman can liquidate home-vault inventory without hauling it to Unicorn Town, and bought goods become instantly eligible for upkeep/bandit theft without a deposit trip. The tests lock this in (`packages/contracts/test/ClanWorld.t.sol:775-888, 1013-1085, 1485-1490`).

Per the Phase 6 market spec, `market_sell` is from worker carry and `market_buy` returns to worker carry. As shipped, a clansman can liquidate home-vault inventory without hauling it to Unicorn Town, and bought goods become instantly eligible for upkeep/bandit theft without a deposit trip. docs/reviews/pr198-r2-codereview-codex-5-5.md:7613:docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-4.md:11441:- `BuildWall` still bypasses the new queue-time reservation model, so wall progression can still happen through the old 1-tick settlement-time debit path. That reopens the vault race this PR is trying to close, and it can also strand reserved `UpgradeWall` funds if a legacy `BuildWall` reaches level 5 first. See [ClanWorld.sol:1670](/home/claude/code/clan-world/packages/contracts/src/ClanWorld.sol:1670), [ClanWorld.sol:765](/home/claude/code/clan-world/packages/contracts/src/ClanWorld.sol:765), [ClanWorld.sol:815](/home/claude/code/clan-world/packages/contracts/src/ClanWorld.sol:815), and [ClanWorld.sol:2138](/home/claude/code/clan-world/packages/contracts/src/ClanWorld.sol:2138).

docs/reviews/pr198-r2-codereview-codex-5-5.md:9030:docs/reviews/pr250-codereview-opus-4-7.md:126:`ClanWorld.sol:565-591`. Vaults are wiped (`vaultWood/Wheat/Fish/Iron = 0`), but per-clansman `carryWood/Wheat/Fish/Iron` on the now-dead clansmen is preserved. Spec is silent on this. Inconsistent with vault wipe; harmless because dead clansmen can't deposit, but a future `getClanFullView` consumer might surface phantom carry. Either wipe or document the invariant.
docs/reviews/pr198-r2-codereview-codex-5-5.md:9033:docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-opus-4-7.md:7:**Verdict: NEEDS FIXES — do not merge as-is.** Phase 8.1–8.4 ship the correct shape (queue-time vault deduction, mission-nonce-bound reservations, score/rank getters), but four HIGH-severity bugs land with this PR — three of which produce permanent, silent resource loss in well-reachable scenarios (cold death, starvation, BuildWall+UpgradeWall race, level-binding mismatch on cancellation). One MEDIUM-severity bug re-violates the #261 derived-getter contract that #230 was supposed to enforce. The score formula bit-packing and `getRankings` sort are correct and well-designed; the bugs are entirely in the reservation/refund lifecycle and in the choice to read raw committed storage from a documented "settled"-named getter. Cost tables match v4 spec §8 line-for-line. Durations diverge from spec ("single-tick") to 2/2/4 — likely intentional but unannounced.
docs/reviews/pr198-r2-codereview-codex-5-5.md:9037:docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-opus-4-7.md:55:2. Clansman B queues `BuildWall`. Validator: only homebase check — passes. **No reservation, no pending bump, no vault deduct.**
docs/reviews/pr198-r2-codereview-codex-5-5.md:9038:docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-opus-4-7.md:56:3. B settles first (duration 1 vs UpgradeWall's 2). `_tryBuildWall`: `nextLevel = 4+1 = 5`, costs 50w+15i, vault has it (UpgradeWall's reservation already accounted), deduct 50w+15i, set wallLevel=5.
docs/reviews/pr198-r2-codereview-codex-5-5.md:9039:docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-opus-4-7.md:91:The PR's own NatSpec on `getClanScore` admits this: *"...does not simulate pending lazy settlement until derived settlement getters are fixed in #230."* But #230 was supposed to be the fix for #261, which #261 in turn closed by simulating settlement in derived getters. Phase 8.4 was explicitly tracked as "uses derived getters per #261 fix" in the review brief. This getter does the opposite.
docs/reviews/pr198-r2-codereview-codex-5-5.md:9042:docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-opus-4-7.md:112:`_clearWallUpgradeReservation` only deletes the reservation struct and decrements the pending counter — it does NOT credit vault. Currently this resource-burn path is unreachable in normal play (validator's pending counter prevents reserving past max with ≤ 4 clansmen per clan), but **Phase 9 attack-resolution will introduce wall-destruction writes**, creating a real race: queue UpgradeWall at L4 → bandit destroys wall to L3 between queue and settle → wall is back at L4 (then settle overwrites with `old+1=4` which is fine actually).
docs/reviews/pr198-r2-codereview-codex-5-5.md:9043:docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-opus-4-7.md:129:### M3 — Cross-type reassignment (e.g. UpgradeWall → UpgradeBase) validates against pre-refund vault, can spuriously reject [`ClanWorld.sol:1233`, `:1251-1268`, `:1806-1828`]
docs/reviews/pr198-r2-codereview-codex-5-5.md:9044:docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-opus-4-7.md:131:`_validateAction` runs at line 1233, BEFORE `_refundWallUpgradeReservation` at line 1252. So switching a clansman from a queued UpgradeWall to a fresh UpgradeBase can return `ERR_MISSING_RESOURCES` even when, after refund, the vault would be sufficient. Workaround is "submit Wait first, then base" — but it's a bad UX seam for Elder agents.
docs/reviews/pr198-r2-codereview-codex-5-5.md:9045:docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-opus-4-7.md:133:**Fix:** in `_validateUpgradeBaseOrder`, also synthesize the vault refund of any active wall/monument reservation for the same clansman. Or refactor: refund FIRST in `_processOrder` at the point of "we're definitely re-tasking", then validate the new action.

docs/reviews/pr198-r2-codereview-codex-5-5.md:9260:docs/reviews/r2-phase6/pr198-codereview-codex-5-5.md:3493:         assertEq(world.getClansman(csId0).carryWood, 0, "deposit settled: carry cleared");
docs/reviews/pr198-r2-codereview-codex-5-5.md:9464:docs/reviews/r2-phase6/pr198-codereview-codex-5-5.md:6057:packages/contracts/src/ClanWorld.sol:3246:                || action == ActionType.UpgradeMonument || action == ActionType.MarketBuy
docs/reviews/pr198-r2-codereview-codex-5-5.md:9722:docs/reviews/r2-phase6/pr198-codereview-codex-5-5.md:10325:- `packages/contracts/src/ClanWorld.sol:1656`, `1771`, `1824`, `1946` — Market trades bypass clansman carry and mutate clan vaults directly. Spec says `MarketSell` source is worker carry and `MarketBuy` destination is worker carry (`docs/planning/clanworld_v4_2_state_schema_interface_spec.md:565-585`). Current code lets a worker in Unicorn Town sell homebase vault resources without carrying them, and bought resources appear in the vault without a return/deposit trip. This also changes bandit loot/target timing because vault value changes immediately.
docs/reviews/pr198-r2-codereview-codex-5-5.md:9723:docs/reviews/r2-phase6/pr198-codereview-codex-5-5.md:10335:- `packages/contracts/test/HeartbeatOrdering.t.sol` encodes the vault-based market model: one clansman deposits into vault and another sells from vault in the same heartbeat. That protects heartbeat ordering, but it masks the carry-accounting regression above.
docs/reviews/pr198-r2-codereview-codex-5-5.md:9725:docs/reviews/r2-phase6/pr198-codereview-codex-5-5.md:10360:- `packages/contracts/src/ClanWorld.sol:1656`, `1771`, `1824`, `1946` — Market trades bypass clansman carry and mutate clan vaults directly. Spec says `MarketSell` source is worker carry and `MarketBuy` destination is worker carry (`docs/planning/clanworld_v4_2_state_schema_interface_spec.md:565-585`). Current code lets a worker in Unicorn Town sell homebase vault resources without carrying them, and bought resources appear in the vault without a return/deposit trip. This also changes bandit loot/target timing because vault value changes immediately.
docs/reviews/pr198-r2-codereview-codex-5-5.md:9726:docs/reviews/r2-phase6/pr198-codereview-codex-5-5.md:10370:- `packages/contracts/test/HeartbeatOrdering.t.sol` encodes the vault-based market model: one clansman deposits into vault and another sells from vault in the same heartbeat. That protects heartbeat ordering, but it masks the carry-accounting regression above.
docs/reviews/pr198-r2-codereview-codex-5-5.md:9741:docs/planning/phase-3-test-spec.md:380:**Setup:** Clan vault has 0 stone. Attempt `BuildWall`.
docs/reviews/pr198-r2-codereview-codex-5-5.md:9745:docs/planning/phase-3-test-spec.md:445:- Both deposit resources into vaults.
docs/reviews/pr198-r2-codereview-codex-5-5.md:10055:docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-codex-5-5.md:9362:  3167	                || action == ActionType.UpgradeMonument || action == ActionType.MarketBuy
docs/reviews/pr198-r2-codereview-codex-5-5.md:10110:docs/reviews/r2-phase6/pr198-codereview-opus-4-7.md:91:- **Heartbeat ordering is correct.** Settle (step 1) → market exec (step 2) → world events (step 4) → tick increment (step 5) is verified by `HeartbeatOrdering.t.sol::test_heartbeat_settlementBeforeMarket` and `test_heartbeat_multipleStepsInOneTick`. Same-tick deposit-then-sell works because deposit's `_addToVault` happens before the queued sell drains the vault.
docs/reviews/pr198-r2-codereview-codex-5-5.md:10203:docs/reviews/pr250-codereview-codex-5-5.md:2353:docs/reviews/pr198-codereview-codex-5-4.md:9421:docs/planning/V1/01 Blockchain Game Spec/clanworld_v4_2_state_schema_interface_spec.md:818:Because upkeep is applied before deposit effects, a worker arriving home with wood/food in carry does **not** save the base from that same tick’s winter burn or upkeep shortage.
docs/reviews/pr198-r2-codereview-codex-5-5.md:10231:docs/reviews/pr250-codereview-codex-5-5.md:3732:docs/reviews/pr250-codereview-codex-5-4.md:3532:docs/reviews/pr198-codereview-codex-5-4.md:9421:docs/planning/V1/01 Blockchain Game Spec/clanworld_v4_2_state_schema_interface_spec.md:818:Because upkeep is applied before deposit effects, a worker arriving home with wood/food in carry does **not** save the base from that same tick’s winter burn or upkeep shortage.
docs/reviews/pr198-r2-codereview-codex-5-5.md:10233:docs/reviews/pr250-codereview-codex-5-5.md:3763:docs/reviews/pr250-codereview-codex-5-4.md:3563:docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:5383:docs/planning/clanworld_v4_2_state_schema_interface_spec.md:818:Because upkeep is applied before deposit effects, a worker arriving home with wood/food in carry does **not** save the base from that same tick’s winter burn or upkeep shortage.

docs/reviews/pr198-r2-codereview-codex-5-5.md:10358:docs/reviews/r2-phase6/pr198-codereview-codex-5-4.md:7639:docs/reviews/pr198-codereview-codex-5-5.md:3451:+    //   - cs0 placed at Unicorn Town, deposits to Forest homebase: settlesAtTick = T0+3
docs/reviews/pr198-r2-codereview-codex-5-5.md:10563:docs/reviews/r2-phase6/pr198-codereview-codex-5-4.md:9421:docs/planning/V1/01 Blockchain Game Spec/clanworld_v4_2_state_schema_interface_spec.md:818:Because upkeep is applied before deposit effects, a worker arriving home with wood/food in carry does **not** save the base from that same tick’s winter burn or upkeep shortage.
docs/reviews/pr198-r2-codereview-codex-5-5.md:10667:docs/reviews/r2-phase6/pr198-codereview-codex-5-4.md:10907:  Market I/O is using the wrong balance domain. Sells read/deduct `clan.vault*`, and buys credit `clan.vault*`, while submission still rejects buys based on worker carry capacity. Per the Phase 6 market spec, `market_sell` is from worker carry and `market_buy` returns to worker carry. As shipped, a clansman can liquidate home-vault inventory without hauling it to Unicorn Town, and bought goods become instantly eligible for upkeep/bandit theft without a deposit trip. The tests lock this in (`packages/contracts/test/ClanWorld.t.sol:775-888, 1013-1085, 1485-1490`).
docs/reviews/pr198-r2-codereview-codex-5-5.md:10668:docs/reviews/r2-phase6/pr198-codereview-codex-5-4.md:10925:- The biggest integrated consequence is cross-phase: routing market buys/sells through vault balances instead of worker carry materially changes Phase 5 deposit logistics and Phase 9 bandit timing.
docs/reviews/pr198-r2-codereview-codex-5-5.md:10670:docs/reviews/r2-phase6/pr198-codereview-codex-5-4.md:10939:  Market I/O is using the wrong balance domain. Sells read/deduct `clan.vault*`, and buys credit `clan.vault*`, while submission still rejects buys based on worker carry capacity. Per the Phase 6 market spec, `market_sell` is from worker carry and `market_buy` returns to worker carry. As shipped, a clansman can liquidate home-vault inventory without hauling it to Unicorn Town, and bought goods become instantly eligible for upkeep/bandit theft without a deposit trip. The tests lock this in (`packages/contracts/test/ClanWorld.t.sol:775-888, 1013-1085, 1485-1490`).

This also changes bandit loot/target timing because vault value changes immediately.
docs/reviews/pr198-r2-codereview-codex-5-5.md:11888:docs/reviews/pr198-r2-codereview-codex-5-4.md:8972:docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-opus-4-7.md:112:`_clearWallUpgradeReservation` only deletes the reservation struct and decrements the pending counter — it does NOT credit vault. Currently this resource-burn path is unreachable in normal play (validator's pending counter prevents reserving past max with ≤ 4 clansmen per clan), but **Phase 9 attack-resolution will introduce wall-destruction writes**, creating a real race: queue UpgradeWall at L4 → bandit destroys wall to L3 between queue and settle → wall is back at L4 (then settle overwrites with `old+1=4` which is fine actually).
docs/reviews/pr198-r2-codereview-codex-5-5.md:11923:docs/reviews/pr198-r2-codereview-codex-5-4.md:9282:docs/reviews/r2-phase6/pr198-codereview-codex-5-4.md:10907:  Market I/O is using the wrong balance domain. Sells read/deduct `clan.vault*`, and buys credit `clan.vault*`, while submission still rejects buys based on worker carry capacity. Per the Phase 6 market spec, `market_sell` is from worker carry and `market_buy` returns to worker carry. As shipped, a clansman can liquidate home-vault inventory without hauling it to Unicorn Town, and bought goods become instantly eligible for upkeep/bandit theft without a deposit trip. The tests lock this in (`packages/contracts/test/ClanWorld.t.sol:775-888, 1013-1085, 1485-1490`).
docs/reviews/pr198-r2-codereview-codex-5-5.md:11924:docs/reviews/pr198-r2-codereview-codex-5-4.md:9284:docs/reviews/r2-phase6/pr198-codereview-codex-5-4.md:10925:- The biggest integrated consequence is cross-phase: routing market buys/sells through vault balances instead of worker carry materially changes Phase 5 deposit logistics and Phase 9 bandit timing.
docs/reviews/pr198-r2-codereview-codex-5-5.md:11926:docs/reviews/pr198-r2-codereview-codex-5-4.md:9286:docs/reviews/r2-phase6/pr198-codereview-codex-5-4.md:10939:  Market I/O is using the wrong balance domain. Sells read/deduct `clan.vault*`, and buys credit `clan.vault*`, while submission still rejects buys based on worker carry capacity. Per the Phase 6 market spec, `market_sell` is from worker carry and `market_buy` returns to worker carry. As shipped, a clansman can liquidate home-vault inventory without hauling it to Unicorn Town, and bought goods become instantly eligible for upkeep/bandit theft without a deposit trip. The tests lock this in (`packages/contracts/test/ClanWorld.t.sol:775-888, 1013-1085, 1485-1490`).

Per the Phase 6 market spec, `market_sell` is from worker carry and `market_buy` returns to worker carry. As shipped, a clansman can liquidate home-vault inventory without hauling it to Unicorn Town, and bought goods become instantly eligible for upkeep/bandit theft without a deposit trip. docs/reviews/pr198-r2-codereview-codex-5-5.md:11996:docs/reviews/pr198-r2-codereview-codex-5-4.md:11013:docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:6250:docs/reviews/pr198-codereview-codex-5-5.md:10325:- `packages/contracts/src/ClanWorld.sol:1656`, `1771`, `1824`, `1946` — Market trades bypass clansman carry and mutate clan vaults directly. Spec says `MarketSell` source is worker carry and `MarketBuy` destination is worker carry (`docs/planning/clanworld_v4_2_state_schema_interface_spec.md:565-585`). Current code lets a worker in Unicorn Town sell homebase vault resources without carrying them, and bought resources appear in the vault without a return/deposit trip. This also changes bandit loot/target timing because vault value changes immediately.

docs/reviews/pr198-r2-codereview-codex-5-5.md:12754:docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:14037:`quoteLootValueSettled()` still returns raw `_lootValueRaw(_clans[clanId])`, and `_getClanScore()` reads `_clans[clanId]` directly. That violates the Phase 8.4 / #261 expectation that leaderboard scoring uses settled/derived getters. A clan with unsettled upkeep, deposits, deaths, or vault changes can be mis-ranked until someone mutates settlement.
docs/reviews/pr198-r2-codereview-codex-5-5.md:12755:docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:14105:`quoteLootValueSettled()` still returns raw `_lootValueRaw(_clans[clanId])`, and `_getClanScore()` reads `_clans[clanId]` directly. A clan with unsettled upkeep, deposits, deaths, or vault changes can be mis-ranked until someone mutates settlement.

docs/reviews/pr198-r2-codereview-codex-5-5.md:14181:   503	2. gather + deposit
docs/reviews/pr198-r2-codereview-codex-5-5.md:14420:   The spec allows moving resources out of vault into worker inventories before bandit targeting, and the prompt explicitly calls out “travels to base, fills carry from vault.” This patch only implements carry-to-vault deposit. There is no reverse vault-to-carry path during order submission, travel settlement, or market preparation; tests mostly bypass this with harness `setCarry`. Starter vault resources therefore cannot be hauled to market or saved from a same-tick bandit raid.  
docs/reviews/pr198-r2-codereview-codex-5-5.md:14466:   The spec allows moving resources out of vault into worker inventories before bandit targeting, and the prompt explicitly calls out “travels to base, fills carry from vault.” This patch only implements carry-to-vault deposit. There is no reverse vault-to-carry path during order submission, travel settlement, or market preparation; tests mostly bypass this with harness `setCarry`. Starter vault resources therefore cannot be hauled to market or saved from a same-tick bandit raid.  
docs/reviews/pr199-r3-codereview-codex-5-5.md:23:Phase 8 release PR (dev-phase-8-buildings → dev). 6 sub-issues already merged + reviewed individually. R1 super-swarm flagged 6 HIGHs (reservation timing, durations off-spec, rank getters stale, BuildWall+UpgradeWall race, death-refund orphan, level-binding mismatch) + wood economy retune outside scope. R2 fix-round (PR #288, just merged) addressed all 6 HIGHs + reverted wood economy. Tests: 118/118 forge passing.

codex
The main bug candidate I’ve found locally is in upgrade replacement across types, and I’m sanity-checking whether it’s isolated or part of a wider validation gap. I’m also checking whether the new rank/simulation getters ever rely on stale storage fields that heartbeat settlement doesn’t keep in sync.

collab: Wait
codex
One explorer just came back with a useful angle: settlement order and reservation order can diverge, which opens both undercharging and “reservation isn’t really a lock” problems. I’m pulling the exact call chain now and waiting on the other two parallel passes before I collapse this into the final merge recommendation.
collab: Wait
collab: Wait
codex
I have enough to know this isn’t a clean merge yet. I’m waiting briefly on the API-surface pass in case it adds one more integration seam, but the current blockers are already in the “real behavior diverges under combined systems” category rather than isolated unit bugs.

  1941	        }
  1942	        if (token == _treasury.ironToken) {
  1943	            clan.vaultIron += amount;
  1944	            return;
  1945	        }
  1946	        if (token == _treasury.wheatToken) {
  1947	            clan.vaultWheat += amount;
  1948	            return;
  1949	        }
  1950	        if (token == _treasury.fishToken) {
  1951	            clan.vaultFish += amount;
  1952	            return;
  1953	        }
  1954	    }
  1955	
  1956	    /// @dev Deduct an amount of a resource token from the clan vault. Returns false if insufficient.
  1957	    function _deductFromVault(Clan storage clan, address token, uint256 amount) internal returns (bool) {
  1958	        if (token == _treasury.woodToken) {
  1959	            if (clan.vaultWood < amount) return false;
  1960	            clan.vaultWood -= amount;
  1961	            return true;
  1962	        }
  1963	        if (token == _treasury.ironToken) {
  1964	            if (clan.vaultIron < amount) return false;
  1965	            clan.vaultIron -= amount;
  1966	            return true;
  1967	        }
  1968	        if (token == _treasury.wheatToken) {
  1969	            if (clan.vaultWheat < amount) return false;
  1970	            clan.vaultWheat -= amount;
  1971	            return true;
  1972	        }
  1973	        if (token == _treasury.fishToken) {
  1974	            if (clan.vaultFish < amount) return false;
  1975	            clan.vaultFish -= amount;
  1976	            return true;
  1977	        }
  1978	        return false;
  1979	    }
  1980	
  1981	    /// @dev Execute a scheduled market sell: deduct resource from vault, credit gold.
  1982	    function _executeMarketSell(
  1983	        uint64 closedTick,
  1984	        uint32 clanId,
  1985	        uint32 clansmanId,
  1986	        address token,
  1987	        uint256 amount,
  1988	        uint64 commitSequence
  1989	    ) internal {
  1990	        if (!_treasury.poolsSeeded) {
  1991	            emit MarketActionFailed(clanId, clansmanId, ActionType.MarketSell, StatusCode.ERR_MARKET_UNSUPPORTED_TOKEN);
  1992	            return;
  1993	        }
  1994	        address poolAddr = _poolFor(token);
  1995	        if (poolAddr == address(0)) {
  1996	            emit MarketActionFailed(clanId, clansmanId, ActionType.MarketSell, StatusCode.ERR_MARKET_UNSUPPORTED_TOKEN);
  1997	            return;
  1998	        }
  1999	
  2000	        Clan storage clan = _clans[clanId];
  2001	        if (!_deductFromVault(clan, token, amount)) {
  2002	            emit MarketActionFailed(clanId, clansmanId, ActionType.MarketSell, StatusCode.ERR_MISSING_RESOURCES);
  2003	            return;
  2004	        }
  2005	
  2006	        uint256 goldOut = StubPool(poolAddr).sellResource(amount);
  2007	        clan.goldBalance += goldOut;
  2008	
  2009	        emit ScheduledMarketActionExecuted(
  2010	            closedTick, commitSequence, clanId, clansmanId, token, _treasury.goldToken, amount, goldOut
  2011	        );
  2012	    }
  2013	
  2014	    /// @dev Execute a scheduled market buy: deduct gold from purse, credit resource to vault.
  2015	    function _executeMarketBuy(
  2016	        uint64 closedTick,
  2017	        uint32 clanId,
  2018	        uint32 clansmanId,
  2019	        address token,
  2020	        uint256 amountOut,
  2021	        uint256 maxGoldIn,
  2022	        uint64 commitSequence
  2023	    ) internal {
  2024	        if (!_treasury.poolsSeeded) {
  2025	            emit MarketActionFailed(clanId, clansmanId, ActionType.MarketBuy, StatusCode.ERR_MARKET_UNSUPPORTED_TOKEN);
  2026	            return;
  2027	        }
  2028	        address poolAddr = _poolFor(token);
  2029	        if (poolAddr == address(0)) {
  2030	            emit MarketActionFailed(clanId, clansmanId, ActionType.MarketBuy, StatusCode.ERR_MARKET_UNSUPPORTED_TOKEN);
  2031	            return;
  2032	        }
  2033	
  2034	        // Quote gold cost without updating reserves
  2035	        uint256 goldIn = StubPool(poolAddr).quoteBuy(amountOut);
  2036	
  2037	        Clan storage clan = _clans[clanId];
  2038	
  2039	        if (goldIn > maxGoldIn) {
  2040	            emit MarketActionFailed(
  2041	                clanId, clansmanId, ActionType.MarketBuy, StatusCode.ERR_MARKET_BUY_MAX_GOLD_EXCEEDED
  2042	            );
  2043	            return;
  2044	        }
  2045	        if (clan.goldBalance < goldIn) {
  2046	            emit MarketActionFailed(clanId, clansmanId, ActionType.MarketBuy, StatusCode.ERR_NOT_ENOUGH_GOLD);
  2047	            return;
  2048	        }
  2049	
  2050	        // Execute — use return value to guard against any future pool divergence
  2051	        uint256 actualGoldIn = StubPool(poolAddr).buyResource(amountOut);
  2052	        clan.goldBalance -= actualGoldIn;
  2053	        _addToVault(clan, token, amountOut);
  2054	
  2055	        emit ScheduledMarketActionExecuted(
  2056	            closedTick, commitSequence, clanId, clansmanId, _treasury.goldToken, token, actualGoldIn, amountOut
  2057	        );
  2058	    }
  2059	
  2060	    function _validateAction(Clan storage clan, Clansman storage cs, ClanOrder calldata order, uint8 gotoRegion)

  2157	    function _validateUpgradeWallOrder(Clan storage clan, uint32 clansmanId) internal view returns (StatusCode) {
  2158	        uint8 pendingUpgrades = _pendingWallUpgradesByClan[clan.clanId];
  2159	        uint256 releasedWood;
  2160	        uint256 releasedIron;
  2161	
  2162	        WallUpgradeReservation storage existing = _wallUpgradeReservations[clansmanId];
  2163	        if (existing.active && existing.clanId == clan.clanId) {
  2164	            pendingUpgrades -= 1;
  2165	            releasedWood = existing.woodCost;
  2166	            releasedIron = existing.ironCost;
  2167	        }
  2168	
  2169	        uint256 availableWood = _spendableAfterReleasing(clan.vaultWood, _reservedWoodByClan[clan.clanId], releasedWood);
  2170	        uint256 availableIron = _spendableAfterReleasing(clan.vaultIron, _reservedIronByClan[clan.clanId], releasedIron);
  2171	
  2172	        uint8 plannedCurrentLevel = clan.wallLevel + pendingUpgrades;
  2173	        if (plannedCurrentLevel >= WALL_MAX_LEVEL) return StatusCode.ERR_INVALID_ACTION;
  2174	
  2175	        (uint256 woodCost, uint256 ironCost) = _wallUpgradeCost(plannedCurrentLevel);
  2176	        if (availableWood < woodCost || availableIron < ironCost) return StatusCode.ERR_MISSING_RESOURCES;
  2177	
  2178	        return StatusCode.OK;
  2179	    }
