  2181	    function _reserveWallUpgrade(Clan storage clan, uint32 clanId, uint32 clansmanId, uint64 missionNonce) internal {
  2182	        uint8 plannedCurrentLevel = clan.wallLevel + _pendingWallUpgradesByClan[clanId];
  2183	        (uint256 woodCost, uint256 ironCost) = _wallUpgradeCost(plannedCurrentLevel);
  2184	
  2185	        _pendingWallUpgradesByClan[clanId] += 1;
  2186	        _reservedWoodByClan[clanId] += woodCost;
  2187	        _reservedIronByClan[clanId] += ironCost;
  2188	
  2189	        _wallUpgradeReservations[clansmanId] = WallUpgradeReservation({
  2190	            active: true,
  2191	            clanId: clanId,
  2192	            missionNonce: missionNonce,
  2193	            fromLevel: plannedCurrentLevel,
  2194	            toLevel: plannedCurrentLevel + 1,
  2195	            woodCost: woodCost,
  2196	            ironCost: ironCost
  2197	        });
  2198	    }

  2200	    function _refundWallUpgradeReservation(uint32 clansmanId) internal {

  2202	        if (!reservation.active) return;

  2206	
  2207	    function _clearWallUpgradeReservation(uint32 clansmanId) internal {
  2208	        WallUpgradeReservation storage reservation = _wallUpgradeReservations[clansmanId];
  2209	        if (!reservation.active) return;
  2210	
  2211	        uint32 clanId = reservation.clanId;
  2212	        if (_pendingWallUpgradesByClan[clanId] > 0) {
  2213	            _pendingWallUpgradesByClan[clanId] -= 1;
  2214	        }
  2215	        _reservedWoodByClan[clanId] = _subtractHeld(_reservedWoodByClan[clanId], reservation.woodCost);
  2216	        _reservedIronByClan[clanId] = _subtractHeld(_reservedIronByClan[clanId], reservation.ironCost);
  2217	
  2218	        delete _wallUpgradeReservations[clansmanId];

  2272	    function _refundBaseUpgradeReservation(uint32 clansmanId) internal {

  2279	    function _clearBaseUpgradeReservation(uint32 clansmanId) internal {

  2281	        if (!reservation.active) return;
  2282	
  2283	        uint32 clanId = reservation.clanId;
  2284	        if (_pendingBaseUpgradesByClan[clanId] > 0) {
  2285	            _pendingBaseUpgradesByClan[clanId] -= 1;
  2286	        }
  2287	        _reservedWoodByClan[clanId] = _subtractHeld(_reservedWoodByClan[clanId], reservation.woodCost);
  2288	        _reservedIronByClan[clanId] = _subtractHeld(_reservedIronByClan[clanId], reservation.ironCost);
  2289	        _reservedWheatByClan[clanId] = _subtractHeld(_reservedWheatByClan[clanId], reservation.wheatCost);
  2290	
  2291	        delete _baseUpgradeReservations[clansmanId];
  2292	    }
  2293	
  2294	    function _validateUpgradeMonumentOrder(Clan storage clan, uint32 clansmanId) internal view returns (StatusCode) {
  2295	        uint8 pendingUpgrades = _pendingMonumentUpgradesByClan[clan.clanId];
  2296	        uint256 releasedWood;
  2297	        uint256 releasedIron;
  2298	        uint256 releasedWheat;
  2299	        uint256 releasedBlueprint;
  2300	
  2301	        MonumentUpgradeReservation storage existing = _monumentUpgradeReservations[clansmanId];
  2302	        if (existing.active && existing.clanId == clan.clanId) {
  2303	            pendingUpgrades -= 1;
  2304	            releasedWood = existing.woodCost;
  2305	            releasedIron = existing.ironCost;
  2306	            releasedWheat = existing.wheatCost;
  2307	            releasedBlueprint = existing.blueprintCost;
  2308	        }
  2309	
  2310	        uint256 availableWood = _spendableAfterReleasing(clan.vaultWood, _reservedWoodByClan[clan.clanId], releasedWood);
  2311	        uint256 availableIron = _spendableAfterReleasing(clan.vaultIron, _reservedIronByClan[clan.clanId], releasedIron);
  2312	        uint256 availableWheat =
  2313	            _spendableAfterReleasing(clan.vaultWheat, _reservedWheatByClan[clan.clanId], releasedWheat);
  2314	        uint256 availableBlueprint =
  2315	            _spendableAfterReleasing(clan.blueprintBalance, _reservedBlueprintByClan[clan.clanId], releasedBlueprint);
  2316	
  2317	        uint8 plannedCurrentLevel = clan.monumentLevel + pendingUpgrades;
  2318	        if (plannedCurrentLevel >= MONUMENT_MAX_LEVEL) return StatusCode.ERR_INVALID_ACTION;
  2319	
  2320	        (uint256 woodCost, uint256 ironCost, uint256 wheatCost, uint256 blueprintCost) =
  2321	            _monumentUpgradeCost(plannedCurrentLevel);
  2322	        if (
  2323	            availableWood < woodCost || availableIron < ironCost || availableWheat < wheatCost
  2324	                || availableBlueprint < blueprintCost
  2325	        ) {
  2326	            return StatusCode.ERR_MISSING_RESOURCES;
  2327	        }
  2328	
  2329	        return StatusCode.OK;
  2330	    }
  2331	
  2332	    function _reserveMonumentUpgrade(Clan storage clan, uint32 clanId, uint32 clansmanId, uint64 missionNonce)
  2333	        internal
  2334	    {
  2335	        uint8 plannedCurrentLevel = clan.monumentLevel + _pendingMonumentUpgradesByClan[clanId];
  2336	        (uint256 woodCost, uint256 ironCost, uint256 wheatCost, uint256 blueprintCost) =
  2337	            _monumentUpgradeCost(plannedCurrentLevel);
  2338	
  2339	        _pendingMonumentUpgradesByClan[clanId] += 1;
  2340	        _reservedWoodByClan[clanId] += woodCost;
  2341	        _reservedIronByClan[clanId] += ironCost;
  2342	        _reservedWheatByClan[clanId] += wheatCost;
  2343	        _reservedBlueprintByClan[clanId] += blueprintCost;
  2344	
  2345	        _monumentUpgradeReservations[clansmanId] = MonumentUpgradeReservation({
  2346	            active: true,
  2347	            clanId: clanId,
  2348	            missionNonce: missionNonce,
  2349	            fromLevel: plannedCurrentLevel,
  2350	            toLevel: plannedCurrentLevel + 1,
  2351	            woodCost: woodCost,
  2352	            ironCost: ironCost,
  2353	            wheatCost: wheatCost,
  2354	            blueprintCost: blueprintCost
  2355	        });

  1820	    /// @dev Execute up to MAX_MARKET_ACTIONS_PER_TICK scheduled market actions for the given tick.
  1821	    ///      Overflow is appended to the next tick to keep heartbeat gas bounded.
  1822	    function _executeScheduledMarketActions(uint64 tick) internal {
  1823	        ScheduledMarketAction[] storage actions = _scheduledMarketActions[tick];
  1824	        uint256 len = actions.length;
  1825	        if (len == 0) return;
  1826	
  1827	        uint256 processCount = len > MAX_MARKET_ACTIONS_PER_TICK ? MAX_MARKET_ACTIONS_PER_TICK : len;
  1828	
  1829	        for (uint256 i = 0; i < processCount; i++) {
  1830	            ScheduledMarketAction storage sma = actions[i];
  1831	
  1832	            // Validate clansman still belongs to the clan
  1833	            Clansman storage cs = _clansmen[sma.clansmanId];
  1834	            if (cs.clanId != sma.clanId || cs.state == ClansmanState.DEAD) {
  1835	                emit MarketActionFailed(sma.clanId, sma.clansmanId, sma.action, StatusCode.ERR_INVALID_CLANSMAN);
  1836	                continue;
  1837	            }
  1838	
  1839	            // Guard: clansman was re-tasked if mission action no longer matches the queued type.
  1840	            // Note: _completeMission sets m.active=false during settlement (by design), so we
  1841	            // cannot use m.active as a validity signal here — check action type and nonce.
  1842	            Mission storage m = _missions[sma.clansmanId];
  1843	            if (m.action != sma.action || m.nonce != sma.missionNonce) {
  1844	                emit MarketActionFailed(sma.clanId, sma.clansmanId, sma.action, StatusCode.ERR_INVALID_ACTION);
  1845	                continue;
  1846	            }
  1847	
  1848	            if (sma.action == ActionType.MarketSell) {
  1849	                try this._executeMarketSellExternal(
  1850	                    tick, sma.clanId, sma.clansmanId, sma.marketToken, sma.marketAmount, sma.commitSequence
  1851	                ) {
  1852	                // success
  1853	                }
  1854	                catch {
  1855	                    emit MarketActionFailed(sma.clanId, sma.clansmanId, sma.action, StatusCode.ERR_INVALID_ACTION);
  1856	                }
  1857	            } else if (sma.action == ActionType.MarketBuy) {
  1858	                try this._executeMarketBuyExternal(
  1859	                    tick,
  1860	                    sma.clanId,
  1861	                    sma.clansmanId,
  1862	                    sma.marketToken,
  1863	                    sma.marketAmount,
  1864	                    sma.maxGoldIn,
  1865	                    sma.commitSequence
  1866	                ) {
  1867	                // success
  1868	                }
  1869	                catch {
  1870	                    emit MarketActionFailed(sma.clanId, sma.clansmanId, sma.action, StatusCode.ERR_INVALID_ACTION);
  1871	                }
  1872	            }
  1873	        }
  1874	
  1875	        if (len > processCount) {
  1876	            ScheduledMarketAction[] storage nextActions = _scheduledMarketActions[tick + 1];
  1877	            for (uint256 i = processCount; i < len; i++) {
  1878	                nextActions.push(actions[i]);
  1879	            }
  1880	            // Invariant: each tick queue executes in global commitSequence order, including
  1881	            // older overflow actions merged into a tick that already has native actions.
  1882	            _sortScheduledMarketActionsByCommitSequence(nextActions);
  1883	        }
  1884	
  1885	        delete _scheduledMarketActions[tick];
  1886	    }
  1887	
  1888	    function _sortScheduledMarketActionsByCommitSequence(ScheduledMarketAction[] storage actions) internal {
  1889	        for (uint256 i = 1; i < actions.length; i++) {
  1890	            ScheduledMarketAction memory key = actions[i];
  1891	            uint256 j = i;
  1892	            while (j > 0 && actions[j - 1].commitSequence > key.commitSequence) {
  1893	                actions[j] = actions[j - 1];
  1894	                j--;
  1895	            }
  1896	            actions[j] = key;
  1897	        }
  1898	    }
  1899	
  1900	    /// @dev External wrapper for _executeMarketSell — enables try/catch from heartbeat loop.
  1901	    function _executeMarketSellExternal(
  1902	        uint64 closedTick,
  1903	        uint32 clanId,
  1904	        uint32 clansmanId,
  1905	        address token,
  1906	        uint256 amount,
  1907	        uint64 commitSequence
  1908	    ) external {
  1909	        require(msg.sender == address(this), "ClanWorld: internal only");
  1910	        _executeMarketSell(closedTick, clanId, clansmanId, token, amount, commitSequence);
  1911	    }
  1912	
  1913	    /// @dev External wrapper for _executeMarketBuy — enables try/catch from heartbeat loop.
  1914	    function _executeMarketBuyExternal(
  1915	        uint64 closedTick,

codex
The API-surface pass landed, and it found a cross-package problem that’s bigger than a local contract bug: the contract structs changed in-place, but the handwritten TS/runner ABIs didn’t move with them. I’m verifying those exact consumers now, because if that holds it’s an immediate merge blocker for the real-chain path.

   240	class StubChainClient implements IChainClient {
   241	  async getCurrentTick(): Promise<Tick> {
   242	    return 0;
   243	  }
   244	  async submitOrders(_clanId: string, _orders: ClanOrder[]): Promise<{ txHash: string }> {
   245	    return { txHash: '0xstub' };
   246	  }
   247	  async getClanFullView(clanId: string): Promise<ClanFullView> {
   248	    return {
   249	      clan: { id: clanId, name: `clan-${clanId}`, treasury: '0' },
   250	      controlledRegions: [],
   251	      pendingOrders: [],

   253	    };
   254	  }
   255	}
   256	
   257	class RealChainClient implements IChainClient {
   258	  private readonly client: ReturnType<typeof createPublicClient>;
   259	  private readonly contractAddress: `0x${string}`;
   260	  private readonly transport: ReturnType<typeof http> | ReturnType<typeof fallback>;
   261	
   262	  constructor() {
   263	    const primaryRpc = readEnv('RPC_URL_PRIMARY');
   264	    const fallbackRpc = readEnv('RPC_URL_FALLBACK');
   265	
   266	    this.transport =
   267	      primaryRpc && fallbackRpc
   268	        ? fallback([http(primaryRpc), http(fallbackRpc)])
   269	        : http(primaryRpc ?? fallbackRpc);
   270	
   271	    this.contractAddress = (readEnv('CLAN_WORLD_CONTRACT_ADDRESS') ??
   272	      DEFAULT_CONTRACT_ADDRESS) as `0x${string}`;
   273	
   274	    this.client = createPublicClient({
   275	      chain: baseSepolia,
   276	      transport: this.transport,
   277	    });
   278	  }
   279	
   280	  async getCurrentTick(): Promise<Tick> {
   281	    const snapshot = await this.client.readContract({
   282	      address: this.contractAddress,
   283	      abi: CLAN_WORLD_ABI,
   284	      functionName: 'getWorldSnapshot',
   285	    });
   286	    return Number(snapshot.currentTick); // safe: tick values are small enough to fit Number precisely in Wave 0
   287	  }
   288	
   289	  async submitOrders(clanId: string, orders: ClanOrder[]): Promise<{ txHash: string }> {
   290	    // Wave 0: single-Elder only — concurrent nonce coordination deferred to Wave 1
   291	    const parsedClanId = parseInt(clanId, 10);
   292	    if (isNaN(parsedClanId) || String(parsedClanId) !== clanId.trim()) {
   293	      throw new Error(`submitOrders: clanId must be a decimal integer, got '${clanId}'`);
   294	    }
   295	
   296	    for (const order of orders) {
   297	      if (order.kind === 'mission') {
   298	        const { clansmanId, gotoRegion, action } = order.payload;
   299	        if (clansmanId === undefined || gotoRegion === undefined || action === undefined) {
   300	          throw new Error(`submitOrders: mission order missing required payload fields (clansmanId, gotoRegion, action)`);
   301	        }
   302	      }
   303	    }
   304	
   305	    const nonMissionOrders = orders.filter(o => o.kind !== 'mission');
   306	    if (nonMissionOrders.length > 0) {
   307	      console.warn(`[RealChainClient] submitOrders: ${nonMissionOrders.length} non-mission order(s) skipped (Wave 0 only supports 'mission' kind)`);
   308	    }
   309	
   310	    const contractOrders = orders
   311	      .filter(o => o.kind === 'mission')
   312	      .map(o => ({
   313	        clansmanId: Number(o.payload.clansmanId),
   314	        gotoRegion: Number(o.payload.gotoRegion),
   315	        action: Number(o.payload.action),
   316	        targetClanId: 0,
   317	        marketToken: '0x0000000000000000000000000000000000000000' as `0x${string}`,
   318	        marketAmount: 0n,
   319	        maxGoldIn: 0n,
   320	      }));
   321	
   322	    if (contractOrders.length === 0) {
   323	      throw new Error('submitOrders: no valid mission orders to submit');
   324	    }
   325	
   326	    const keyPath = readEnv('ELDER_WALLET_KEY_PATH');
   327	    let pk: string | undefined;
   328	    let pkSource: string | undefined;
   329	    if (keyPath) {
   330	      try {
   331	        pk = fs.readFileSync(keyPath, 'utf8').trim();
   332	        pkSource = `ELDER_WALLET_KEY_PATH file at ${keyPath}`;
   333	      } catch (err) {
   334	        if (err && typeof err === 'object' && 'code' in err && err.code === 'ENOENT') {
   335	          throw new Error(
   336	            `ELDER_WALLET_KEY_PATH file not found at ${keyPath}; either set DEPLOYER_PRIVATE_KEY env var or provide a key file`,
   337	          );
   338	        }
   339	        const msg = err instanceof Error ? err.message : String(err);
   340	        throw new Error(`Failed to read ELDER_WALLET_KEY_PATH file at ${keyPath}: ${msg}`);
   341	      }
   342	    } else {
   343	      const fallbackKey = readEnv('DEPLOYER_PRIVATE_KEY');
   344	      if (fallbackKey) {
   345	        console.warn('[RealChainClient] ELDER_WALLET_KEY_PATH not set; falling back to DEPLOYER_PRIVATE_KEY (deprecated)');
   346	        pk = fallbackKey;
   347	        pkSource = 'DEPLOYER_PRIVATE_KEY env var';
   348	      }
   349	    }
   350	    if (!pk) throw new Error('Neither ELDER_WALLET_KEY_PATH nor DEPLOYER_PRIVATE_KEY is set');
   351	
   352	    // Normalize: add 0x prefix if missing
   353	    if (!pk.startsWith('0x')) pk = '0x' + pk;
   354	    if (!/^0x[0-9a-fA-F]{64}$/.test(pk)) {
   355	      throw new Error(
   356	        `Invalid private key from ${pkSource ?? 'unknown source'}: expected a 64-hex-char private key (0x-prefixed optional)`,
   357	      );
   358	    }
   359	
   360	    const account = privateKeyToAccount(pk as `0x${string}`);
   361	    const walletClient = createWalletClient({
   362	      account,
   363	      chain: baseSepolia,
   364	      transport: this.transport,
   365	    });
   366	
   367	    const hash = await walletClient.writeContract({
   368	      address: this.contractAddress,
   369	      abi: CLAN_WORLD_ABI,
   370	      functionName: 'submitClanOrders',
   371	      args: [parsedClanId, contractOrders],
   372	    });
   373	
   374	    return { txHash: hash };
   375	  }
   376	
   377	  async getClanFullView(clanId: string): Promise<ClanFullView> {
   378	    const result = await this.client.readContract({
   379	      address: this.contractAddress,
   380	      abi: CLAN_WORLD_ABI,
   381	      functionName: 'getClanFullView',
   382	      args: [parseInt(clanId, 10)],
   383	    });
   384	
   385	    const inner = result.clan.clan;
   386	    return {
   387	      clan: {
   388	        id: String(inner.clanId),
   389	        name: `clan-${inner.clanId}`,
   390	        treasury: String(inner.goldBalance),
   391	      },
   392	      // controlledRegions, pendingOrders, whispers not available in Wave 0 contract read
   393	      controlledRegions: [], // Wave 0: omit base region from controlledRegions; populated in Wave 1 from on-chain data
   394	      pendingOrders: [],
   395	      whispers: [],
   396	    };
   397	  }
   398	}
   399	
   400	export function createChainClient(): IChainClient {
   401	  return readEnv('CLAN_WORLD_USE_STUB_CHAIN') === 'true'
   402	    ? new StubChainClient()
   403	    : new RealChainClient();
   404	}

     2	  ContractFunctionRevertedError,
     3	  createPublicClient,
     4	  createWalletClient,
     5	  http,
     6	  type Account,
     7	  type PublicClient,
     8	  type WalletClient,
     9	} from 'viem';
    10	import { privateKeyToAccount } from 'viem/accounts';
    11	import { baseSepolia } from '@clan-world/shared/adapters';
    12	import {
    13	  HeartbeatRateLimitedError,
    14	  type IHeartbeatCaller,
    15	} from '@clan-world/agents/seams';
    16	
    17	/**
    18	 * Minimal ABI: only the `heartbeat()` write and the `nextHeartbeatAtTs` field
    19	 * we read out of `getWorldState()`. We avoid pulling in the full IClanWorld
    20	 * ABI here so the runner stays decoupled from contract-package versioning.
    21	 */
    22	const HEARTBEAT_ABI = [
    23	  {
    24	    type: 'function',
    25	    name: 'heartbeat',
    26	    inputs: [],
    27	    outputs: [],
    28	    stateMutability: 'nonpayable',
    29	  },
    30	  {
    31	    type: 'function',
    32	    name: 'getWorldState',
    33	    inputs: [],
    34	    outputs: [
    35	      {
    36	        name: '',
    37	        type: 'tuple',
    38	        components: [
    39	          { name: 'currentTick', type: 'uint64' },
    40	          { name: 'seasonStartTick', type: 'uint64' },
    41	          { name: 'seasonEndTick', type: 'uint64' },
    42	          { name: 'seasonFinalized', type: 'bool' },
    43	          { name: 'nextHeartbeatAtTs', type: 'uint64' },
    44	          { name: 'nextBanditSpawnEligibleTick', type: 'uint64' },
    45	          { name: 'currentBanditSpawnChanceBps', type: 'uint16' },
    46	          { name: 'currentTickSeed', type: 'bytes32' },
    47	          { name: 'activeBanditId', type: 'uint32' },
    48	          { name: 'winterActive', type: 'bool' },
    49	          { name: 'winterStartsAtTick', type: 'uint64' },
    50	          { name: 'winterEndsAtTick', type: 'uint64' },
    51	          { name: 'nextCommitSequence', type: 'uint64' },
    52	        ],
    53	      },
    54	    ],
    55	    stateMutability: 'view',
    56	  },
    57	] as const;
    58	
    59	export interface RunnerHeartbeatConfig {
    60	  /** Hex-encoded 64-char private key, optionally 0x-prefixed. */
    61	  privateKey: string;
    62	  /** Override RPC URL; defaults to the Base Sepolia public endpoint. */
    63	  rpcUrl?: string;
    64	  /** ClanWorld contract address. */
    65	  contractAddress: `0x${string}`;
    66	}
    67	
    68	/**
    69	 * Reads `RUNNER_PRIVATE_KEY`, `RPC_URL_PRIMARY`, `CLAN_WORLD_CONTRACT_ADDRESS`
    70	 * from env. Throws if `RUNNER_PRIVATE_KEY` is missing — the runner intentionally
    71	 * does not generate or store its own wallet; provisioning is operator-side.
    72	 */
    73	export function configFromEnv(env: NodeJS.ProcessEnv = process.env): RunnerHeartbeatConfig {
    74	  const pk = env['RUNNER_PRIVATE_KEY'];
    75	  if (!pk) {
    76	    throw new Error(
    77	      'RUNNER_PRIVATE_KEY is not set — the runner needs a dedicated wallet (NEVER reuse an Elder wallet). ' +
    78	        'Provision a fresh key, fund it with testnet ETH, and export RUNNER_PRIVATE_KEY before starting the daemon.',
    79	    );
    80	  }
    81	  const contractAddress = env['CLAN_WORLD_CONTRACT_ADDRESS'];
    82	  if (!contractAddress || !/^0x[0-9a-fA-F]{40}$/.test(contractAddress)) {
    83	    throw new Error(
    84	      `CLAN_WORLD_CONTRACT_ADDRESS missing or invalid; expected 0x-prefixed 40-hex-char address, got ${String(contractAddress)}`,
    85	    );
    86	  }
    87	  return {
    88	    privateKey: pk,
    89	    rpcUrl: env['RPC_URL_PRIMARY'] || env['RPC_URL_FALLBACK'],
    90	    contractAddress: contractAddress as `0x${string}`,
    91	  };
    92	}
    93	
    94	/**
    95	 * Viem-backed `IHeartbeatCaller`. Wallet account is the dedicated runner key.
    96	 *
    97	 * Rate-limit detection: ClanWorld.heartbeat() reverts when called before
    98	 * `nextHeartbeatAtTs`. We don't have a typed custom error in the ABI, so on
    99	 * any revert we re-read `getWorldState().nextHeartbeatAtTs` and, if it is
   100	 * still in the future, throw `HeartbeatRateLimitedError(nextAllowedAt)`.
   101	 * Other reverts surface as the original error.
   102	 */
   103	export class RunnerCastHeartbeat implements IHeartbeatCaller {
   104	  private readonly publicClient: PublicClient;
   105	  private readonly walletClient: WalletClient;
   106	  private readonly account: Account;
   107	  private readonly contractAddress: `0x${string}`;
   108	
   109	  constructor(cfg: RunnerHeartbeatConfig) {
   110	    const pk = normalizePk(cfg.privateKey);
   111	    this.account = privateKeyToAccount(pk);
   112	    const transport = cfg.rpcUrl ? http(cfg.rpcUrl) : http();
   113	    this.publicClient = createPublicClient({ chain: baseSepolia, transport });
   114	    this.walletClient = createWalletClient({
   115	      account: this.account,
   116	      chain: baseSepolia,
   117	      transport,
   118	    });
   119	    this.contractAddress = cfg.contractAddress;
   120	  }
   121	
   122	  async callHeartbeat(): Promise<{ txHash: string }> {
   123	    try {
   124	      const hash = await this.walletClient.writeContract({
   125	        account: this.account,
   126	        chain: baseSepolia,
   127	        address: this.contractAddress,
   128	        abi: HEARTBEAT_ABI,
   129	        functionName: 'heartbeat',
   130	        args: [],
   131	      });
   132	      // Wait for confirmation per the seam contract ("not fire-and-forget").
   133	      const receipt = await this.publicClient.waitForTransactionReceipt({ hash });
   134	      if (receipt.status !== 'success') {
   135	        // Mined-but-reverted. Most common cause is the rate-limit window
   136	        // hadn't elapsed yet (when simulation succeeded but execution didn't).
   137	        // Re-read state to upgrade to HeartbeatRateLimitedError when applicable.
   138	        const next = await this.readNextHeartbeatAt().catch(() => undefined);
   139	        if (next !== undefined && next > Math.floor(Date.now() / 1000)) {
   140	          throw new HeartbeatRateLimitedError(next);
   141	        }
   142	        throw new Error(`heartbeat tx ${hash} reverted on-chain`);
   143	      }
   144	      return { txHash: hash };
   145	    } catch (err) {
   146	      // Already a rate-limit error — rethrow immediately; no second RPC read.
   147	      if (err instanceof HeartbeatRateLimitedError) throw err;
   148	      // Attempt to upgrade only simulation-level contract reverts to
   149	      // HeartbeatRateLimitedError; pre-flight/RPC errors must surface unchanged.
   150	      if (!(err instanceof ContractFunctionRevertedError)) throw err;
   151	      const next = await this.readNextHeartbeatAt().catch(() => undefined);
   152	      if (next !== undefined && next > Math.floor(Date.now() / 1000)) {
   153	        throw new HeartbeatRateLimitedError(next);
   154	      }
   155	      throw err;
   156	    }
   157	  }
   158	
   159	  async isHeartbeatDue(): Promise<boolean> {
   160	    const next = await this.readNextHeartbeatAt();
   161	    return next <= Math.floor(Date.now() / 1000);
   162	  }
   163	
   164	  private async readNextHeartbeatAt(): Promise<number> {
   165	    const state = await this.publicClient.readContract({
   166	      address: this.contractAddress,
   167	      abi: HEARTBEAT_ABI,
   168	      functionName: 'getWorldState',
   169	      args: [],
   170	    });
   171	    // viem decodes the named tuple into an object with the same field names.
   172	    return Number((state as { nextHeartbeatAtTs: bigint }).nextHeartbeatAtTs);
   173	  }
   174	}
   175	
   176	function normalizePk(pk: string): `0x${string}` {
   177	  const trimmed = pk.trim();
   178	  const withPrefix = trimmed.startsWith('0x') ? trimmed : `0x${trimmed}`;
   179	  if (!/^0x[0-9a-fA-F]{64}$/.test(withPrefix)) {
   180	    throw new Error(
   181	      'RUNNER_PRIVATE_KEY is not a valid 64-hex-char private key (0x-prefixed optional)',
   182	    );
   183	  }
   184	  return withPrefix as `0x${string}`;
   185	}

     1	import type { ClanOrder } from '@clan-world/shared';
     2	import { createChainClient, createConvexClient } from '@clan-world/shared/adapters';
     3	
     4	const CLAN_ID = process.env.CLAN_ID || '1';
     5	
     6	async function main(): Promise<void> {
     7	  const chain = createChainClient();
     8	  const convex = createConvexClient();
     9	
    10	  // Get current tick
    11	  const tick = await chain.getCurrentTick();
    12	  console.error(`[orchestrator] tick=${tick}`);
    13	
    14	  // Hardcoded mission for clan-1: clansman 1 chops wood in Forest
    15	  const orders: ClanOrder[] = [
    16	    {
    17	      kind: 'mission',
    18	      payload: { clansmanId: 1, gotoRegion: 0, action: 1 }, // action=1 is ChopWood
    19	    },
    20	  ];
    21	
    22	  console.error(`[orchestrator] submitting orders for clan-${CLAN_ID}...`);
    23	  const { txHash } = await chain.submitOrders(CLAN_ID, orders);
    24	  console.error(`[orchestrator] tx submitted: ${txHash}`);
    25	
    26	  try {
    27	    await convex.postLog('info', `Elder Aldric (clan-${CLAN_ID}): ChopWood submitted — txHash=${txHash} tick=${tick}`);
    28	  } catch (err) {
    29	    console.error('[orchestrator] convex log failed (non-fatal):', err);
    30	  }
    31	
    32	  // Print final JSON summary to stdout
    33	  process.stdout.write(
    34	    JSON.stringify({ tick, txHash, clan: CLAN_ID, mission: 'ChopWood-Forest' }, null, 2) + '\n',
    35	  );
    36	}
    37	
    38	main().catch(err => {
    39	  console.error('[orchestrator] fatal:', err);
    40	  process.exit(1);
    41	});

codex
# Phase Super-Swarm Review (R3) — PR #199 (head 36420da)

## SUMMARY
NEEDS_FIXES. The contract-level Phase 8 logic is not merge-safe yet: the new reservation model can still be undercharged or bypassed across integrated paths, the new settled-ranking/simulation reads diverge from heartbeat settlement order, and the real-chain TS consumers are now out of sync with the contract ABI. I would not merge this until the pricing/settlement invariants and cross-package ABI drift are fixed.

## HIGH severity findings
- `packages/shared/src/adapters/IChainClient.ts:24`, `packages/shared/src/adapters/IChainClient.ts:280`, `packages/shared/src/adapters/IChainClient.ts:377`, `packages/contracts/src/IClanWorld.sol:189`, `packages/contracts/src/IClanWorld.sol:277`, `packages/contracts/src/IClanWorld.sol:411`, `apps/orchestrator/src/main.ts:11`  
  The handwritten viem ABI in `IChainClient` is still using the pre-Phase-8 tuple layouts. `WorldSnapshot` now has `currentSeasonNumber` and `nextHeartbeatAtTick` inserted before the winter fields, and `Mission` now has `submittedAtTick` / `executesAtTick` / `settlesAtTick` inserted before `clansmanId`. Because viem tuple decoding is positional, `getWorldSnapshot()` / `getClanFullView()` on the real contract can now misdecode or throw, and the orchestrator already calls `getCurrentTick()` on startup. Suggested fix: stop hand-maintaining these tuple ABIs, or at minimum update them to match the canonical contract ABI and add a cross-package decode smoke test.

- `packages/runner/src/runnerCastHeartbeat.ts:22`, `packages/runner/src/runnerCastHeartbeat.ts:164`, `packages/contracts/src/IClanWorld.sol:189`  
  The runner’s minimal `getWorldState()` ABI is also stale. `WorldState` now inserts `currentSeasonNumber` and `nextHeartbeatAtTick` before `nextHeartbeatAtTs`, so `readNextHeartbeatAt()` is reading the wrong slot. That breaks both `isHeartbeatDue()` and the fallback path that converts heartbeat reverts into `HeartbeatRateLimitedError`. Suggested fix: update the tuple shape here as well, or import a shared canonical ABI fragment instead of duplicating it.

- `packages/contracts/src/ClanWorld.sol:1356`, `packages/contracts/src/ClanWorld.sol:799`, `packages/contracts/src/ClanWorld.sol:829`, `packages/contracts/src/ClanWorld.sol:861`, `packages/contracts/src/ClanWorld.sol:2181`, `packages/contracts/src/ClanWorld.sol:2251`, `packages/contracts/src/ClanWorld.sol:2332`  
  Upgrade costs are assigned in submission order, but settlement runs in fixed `clanClansmanIds` order and debits `min(heldCost, currentLevelCost)`. That lets a later, more expensive reservation settle first and pay the cheaper current-level price, while the earlier reservation is capped by its older held cost. Example: queue wall upgrades on `cs2` then `cs1`; if `cs1` settles first, the clan can reach wall level 2 while paying `20 + 20` instead of `20 + 35`. Suggested fix: bind settlement to the reserved level sequence, not the clansman iteration order; e.g. settle in reservation order, or require `currentLevel == reservation.fromLevel` before applying that reservation.

- `packages/contracts/src/ClanWorld.sol:448`, `packages/contracts/src/ClanWorld.sol:1956`, `packages/contracts/src/ClanWorld.sol:2157`, `packages/contracts/src/ClanWorld.sol:2221`, `packages/contracts/src/ClanWorld.sol:2294`  
  The new `_reserved*ByClan` balances only affect order validation. `_applyUpkeep()` and `_deductFromVault()` still spend raw vault balances, so a queued upgrade does not actually lock its resources against upkeep or market sells. That leaves the same TOCTOU class the reservation fix was meant to remove: an upgrade can validate successfully, then fail at settlement because the “held” wheat/wood/iron was consumed elsewhere first. Suggested fix: either escrow/debit reserved resources at queue time, or make every other vault consumer spend only from `vault - reserved`.

- `packages/contracts/src/ClanWorld.sol:405`, `packages/contracts/src/ClanWorld.sol:1326`, `packages/contracts/src/ClanWorld.sol:922`, `packages/contracts/src/ClanWorld.sol:2719`  
  Heartbeat eagerly settles due missions on the closed tick, but it does not advance `lastSettledTick` or apply that tick’s upkeep/regrowth. Later, `_settleClan()` and `_simulateSettleToTick()` replay from the stale checkpoint with upkeep-before-mission ordering. That means a tick-`T` deposit or upgrade can be applied to storage first, then have tick-`T` upkeep replayed against the already-mutated vault, which makes `quoteLootValueSettled()` / `getClanScore()` / `getRankings()` diverge from the actual heartbeat path. Suggested fix: make heartbeat advance the same clan settlement checkpoint/order that lazy settlement and simulation use, or track and skip eagerly-settled ticks so they are not replayed.

## MEDIUM severity findings
- `packages/contracts/src/ClanWorld.sol:1637`, `packages/contracts/src/ClanWorld.sol:1656`, `packages/contracts/src/ClanWorld.sol:2157`, `packages/contracts/src/ClanWorld.sol:2221`, `packages/contracts/src/ClanWorld.sol:2294`  
  Replacing one upgrade type with another on the same clansman can be falsely rejected. Validation only “releases” an existing reservation of the same upgrade type, but the old reservation is not actually refunded until after validation passes. So a worker reserved for `UpgradeWall` may be blocked from switching directly to `UpgradeBase` even when total clan resources are sufficient once the wall reservation is removed. Suggested fix: clear any active upgrade reservation for that clansman before validating the replacement order, or make validation release whichever upgrade reservation is currently attached.

- `packages/contracts/src/IClanWorld.sol:346`, `packages/contracts/src/IClanWorld.sol:743`, `packages/contracts/src/ClanWorld.sol:2666`  
  The interface says `getDerivedClanState()` / `getDerivedClansmanState()` are read-only simulation-forwarded, settled-to-current-tick views, but the implementation still returns mostly raw storage plus a thin live starvation/arrival check. That means callers can receive materially stale derived state for upkeep, deposits, mission completion, plot regrowth, and worker transitions. Suggested fix: either back these getters with `_simulateSettleToTick()` or relax the interface/docs so they no longer promise settled-forward semantics.

- `packages/contracts/src/IClanWorld.sol:776`, `packages/contracts/src/ClanWorld.sol:2839`, `packages/contracts/src/ClanWorld.sol:2876`  
  `getWorldSnapshot()` and `getClanFullView()` now disagree with the new settled ranking getters and with their own interface comments. They bypass the simulator and read raw last-settled storage, while `getClanScore()` / `getRankings()` use read-only settlement simulation. Suggested fix: compose these aggregators from the same derived/simulated state, or explicitly document them as raw snapshots so indexers do not mix incompatible views in the same block.

- `packages/contracts/src/IClanWorld.sol:130`, `packages/contracts/src/ClanWorld.sol:2074`, `packages/shared/src/adapters/IChainClient.ts:310`  
  `BuildWall` is still a public `ActionType` enum value, but the implementation now hard-rejects it and only `UpgradeWall` is valid. Any stale caller still emitting the old numeric code will only discover the break at runtime via `ERR_INVALID_ACTION`. Suggested fix: migrate upstream callers and add adapter-side guards, or remove/rename the deprecated path more explicitly.

## LOW severity findings
CLEAN — no findings.

## Cross-cutting observations
The common root problem is that Phase 8 now has three different notions of “settled” state: heartbeat-mutated storage, lazy settlement, and read-only simulation. Once rankings started depending on simulation, that inconsistency became a shipping bug instead of just an internal quirk.

The added tests are good single-surface coverage, but the missing seams are exactly where the bugs are: out-of-order multi-builder settlement, reserved-vs-upkeep/market interaction, cross-type upgrade replacement, and ABI compatibility checks for `packages/shared` / `packages/runner`.

I couldn’t rerun the reported `forge` suite here because `forge` is not installed in this environment, so this review is based on static tracing of the repo and the integrated call paths.

# Phase Super-Swarm Review (R3) — PR #199 (head 36420da)

## SUMMARY
NEEDS_FIXES. The contract-level Phase 8 logic is not merge-safe yet: the new reservation model can still be undercharged or bypassed across integrated paths, the new settled-ranking/simulation reads diverge from heartbeat settlement order, and the real-chain TS consumers are now out of sync with the contract ABI. I would not merge this until the pricing/settlement invariants and cross-package ABI drift are fixed.

## HIGH severity findings
- `packages/shared/src/adapters/IChainClient.ts:24`, `packages/shared/src/adapters/IChainClient.ts:280`, `packages/shared/src/adapters/IChainClient.ts:377`, `packages/contracts/src/IClanWorld.sol:189`, `packages/contracts/src/IClanWorld.sol:277`, `packages/contracts/src/IClanWorld.sol:411`, `apps/orchestrator/src/main.ts:11`  
  The handwritten viem ABI in `IChainClient` is still using the pre-Phase-8 tuple layouts. `WorldSnapshot` now has `currentSeasonNumber` and `nextHeartbeatAtTick` inserted before the winter fields, and `Mission` now has `submittedAtTick` / `executesAtTick` / `settlesAtTick` inserted before `clansmanId`. Because viem tuple decoding is positional, `getWorldSnapshot()` / `getClanFullView()` on the real contract can now misdecode or throw, and the orchestrator already calls `getCurrentTick()` on startup. Suggested fix: stop hand-maintaining these tuple ABIs, or at minimum update them to match the canonical contract ABI and add a cross-package decode smoke test.

- `packages/runner/src/runnerCastHeartbeat.ts:22`, `packages/runner/src/runnerCastHeartbeat.ts:164`, `packages/contracts/src/IClanWorld.sol:189`  
  The runner’s minimal `getWorldState()` ABI is also stale. `WorldState` now inserts `currentSeasonNumber` and `nextHeartbeatAtTick` before `nextHeartbeatAtTs`, so `readNextHeartbeatAt()` is reading the wrong slot. That breaks both `isHeartbeatDue()` and the fallback path that converts heartbeat reverts into `HeartbeatRateLimitedError`. Suggested fix: update the tuple shape here as well, or import a shared canonical ABI fragment instead of duplicating it.

- `packages/contracts/src/ClanWorld.sol:1356`, `packages/contracts/src/ClanWorld.sol:799`, `packages/contracts/src/ClanWorld.sol:829`, `packages/contracts/src/ClanWorld.sol:861`, `packages/contracts/src/ClanWorld.sol:2181`, `packages/contracts/src/ClanWorld.sol:2251`, `packages/contracts/src/ClanWorld.sol:2332`  
  Upgrade costs are assigned in submission order, but settlement runs in fixed `clanClansmanIds` order and debits `min(heldCost, currentLevelCost)`. That lets a later, more expensive reservation settle first and pay the cheaper current-level price, while the earlier reservation is capped by its older held cost. Example: queue wall upgrades on `cs2` then `cs1`; if `cs1` settles first, the clan can reach wall level 2 while paying `20 + 20` instead of `20 + 35`. Suggested fix: bind settlement to the reserved level sequence, not the clansman iteration order; e.g. settle in reservation order, or require `currentLevel == reservation.fromLevel` before applying that reservation.

- `packages/contracts/src/ClanWorld.sol:448`, `packages/contracts/src/ClanWorld.sol:1956`, `packages/contracts/src/ClanWorld.sol:2157`, `packages/contracts/src/ClanWorld.sol:2221`, `packages/contracts/src/ClanWorld.sol:2294`  
  The new `_reserved*ByClan` balances only affect order validation. `_applyUpkeep()` and `_deductFromVault()` still spend raw vault balances, so a queued upgrade does not actually lock its resources against upkeep or market sells. That leaves the same TOCTOU class the reservation fix was meant to remove: an upgrade can validate successfully, then fail at settlement because the “held” wheat/wood/iron was consumed elsewhere first. Suggested fix: either escrow/debit reserved resources at queue time, or make every other vault consumer spend only from `vault - reserved`.

- `packages/contracts/src/ClanWorld.sol:405`, `packages/contracts/src/ClanWorld.sol:1326`, `packages/contracts/src/ClanWorld.sol:922`, `packages/contracts/src/ClanWorld.sol:2719`  
  Heartbeat eagerly settles due missions on the closed tick, but it does not advance `lastSettledTick` or apply that tick’s upkeep/regrowth. Later, `_settleClan()` and `_simulateSettleToTick()` replay from the stale checkpoint with upkeep-before-mission ordering. That means a tick-`T` deposit or upgrade can be applied to storage first, then have tick-`T` upkeep replayed against the already-mutated vault, which makes `quoteLootValueSettled()` / `getClanScore()` / `getRankings()` diverge from the actual heartbeat path. Suggested fix: make heartbeat advance the same clan settlement checkpoint/order that lazy settlement and simulation use, or track and skip eagerly-settled ticks so they are not replayed.

## MEDIUM severity findings
- `packages/contracts/src/ClanWorld.sol:1637`, `packages/contracts/src/ClanWorld.sol:1656`, `packages/contracts/src/ClanWorld.sol:2157`, `packages/contracts/src/ClanWorld.sol:2221`, `packages/contracts/src/ClanWorld.sol:2294`  
  Replacing one upgrade type with another on the same clansman can be falsely rejected. Validation only “releases” an existing reservation of the same upgrade type, but the old reservation is not actually refunded until after validation passes. So a worker reserved for `UpgradeWall` may be blocked from switching directly to `UpgradeBase` even when total clan resources are sufficient once the wall reservation is removed. Suggested fix: clear any active upgrade reservation for that clansman before validating the replacement order, or make validation release whichever upgrade reservation is currently attached.

- `packages/contracts/src/IClanWorld.sol:346`, `packages/contracts/src/IClanWorld.sol:743`, `packages/contracts/src/ClanWorld.sol:2666`  
  The interface says `getDerivedClanState()` / `getDerivedClansmanState()` are read-only simulation-forwarded, settled-to-current-tick views, but the implementation still returns mostly raw storage plus a thin live starvation/arrival check. That means callers can receive materially stale derived state for upkeep, deposits, mission completion, plot regrowth, and worker transitions. Suggested fix: either back these getters with `_simulateSettleToTick()` or relax the interface/docs so they no longer promise settled-forward semantics.

- `packages/contracts/src/IClanWorld.sol:776`, `packages/contracts/src/ClanWorld.sol:2839`, `packages/contracts/src/ClanWorld.sol:2876`  
  `getWorldSnapshot()` and `getClanFullView()` now disagree with the new settled ranking getters and with their own interface comments. They bypass the simulator and read raw last-settled storage, while `getClanScore()` / `getRankings()` use read-only settlement simulation. Suggested fix: compose these aggregators from the same derived/simulated state, or explicitly document them as raw snapshots so indexers do not mix incompatible views in the same block.

- `packages/contracts/src/IClanWorld.sol:130`, `packages/contracts/src/ClanWorld.sol:2074`, `packages/shared/src/adapters/IChainClient.ts:310`  
  `BuildWall` is still a public `ActionType` enum value, but the implementation now hard-rejects it and only `UpgradeWall` is valid. Any stale caller still emitting the old numeric code will only discover the break at runtime via `ERR_INVALID_ACTION`. Suggested fix: migrate upstream callers and add adapter-side guards, or remove/rename the deprecated path more explicitly.

## LOW severity findings
CLEAN — no findings.

## Cross-cutting observations
The common root problem is that Phase 8 now has three different notions of “settled” state: heartbeat-mutated storage, lazy settlement, and read-only simulation. Once rankings started depending on simulation, that inconsistency became a shipping bug instead of just an internal quirk.

The added tests are good single-surface coverage, but the missing seams are exactly where the bugs are: out-of-order multi-builder settlement, reserved-vs-upkeep/market interaction, cross-type upgrade replacement, and ABI compatibility checks for `packages/shared` / `packages/runner`.

I couldn’t rerun the reported `forge` suite here because `forge` is not installed in this environment, so this review is based on static tracing of the repo and the integrated call paths.
